# SQL helpers: haversine miles + within_radius + useful CTE patterns
sql_helpers = r"""
-- Step 7 SQL helpers for Metrics Contract v1
-- Includes distance functions and reusable helpers.

BEGIN;

-- Haversine distance in meters
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION pt_haversine_meters(lat1 double precision, lon1 double precision, lat2 double precision, lon2 double precision)
RETURNS double precision
LANGUAGE sql
IMMUTABLE
AS $$
  SELECT 2 * 6371000 * asin(
    sqrt(
      pow(sin(radians((lat2 - lat1) / 2)), 2) +
      cos(radians(lat1)) * cos(radians(lat2)) * pow(sin(radians((lon2 - lon1) / 2)), 2)
    )
  );
$$;

-- Haversine distance in miles
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION pt_haversine_miles(lat1 double precision, lon1 double precision, lat2 double precision, lon2 double precision)
RETURNS double precision
LANGUAGE sql
IMMUTABLE
AS $$
  SELECT pt_haversine_meters(lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2) / 1609.344;
$$;

-- Within radius meters
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION pt_within_radius_meters(lat1 double precision, lon1 double precision, lat2 double precision, lon2 double precision, radius_m double precision)
RETURNS boolean
LANGUAGE sql
IMMUTABLE
AS $$
  SELECT pt_haversine_meters(lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2) <= radius_m;
$$;

COMMIT;
""".strip()+"\n"
(sql_dir/"step7_sql_helpers.sql").write_text(sql_helpers)

# Full SqlMetricProvider implementation (TypeScript)
provider = r"""
import { MetricProvider, MetricValue, Window } from "../../puretask_gamification_step5/src/types";
import { withClient } from "../db/client";

/**
 * SqlMetricProvider (FULL, v1)
 * Implements every metric in metrics_contract_v1.json.
 *
 * IMPORTANT ASSUMPTIONS (sane defaults):
 * - Domain tables exist: jobs, ratings, disputes, messages, tips, job_requests, cleaner_level_status, cleaner_settings.
 * - Event log exists: pt_event_log, pt_engagement_sessions, pt_engagement_actions (from Step 4).
 * - Jobs table contains job location + schedule: jobs.lat, jobs.lng, jobs.scheduled_start_at, jobs.completed_at, jobs.status, jobs.cleaning_type, jobs.time_slot, jobs.client_id
 * - If a domain table doesn't exist yet, we fall back to pt_event_log where possible; otherwise return null/0 with TODO.
 *
 * This is designed to be replaced/optimized (materialized views, caches) once shipped.
 */

type AnyJson = Record<string, any>;

export class SqlMetricProvider implements MetricProvider {
  async getMetric(params: {
    cleaner_id: string;
    metric_key: string;
    window?: Window | null;
    filters?: AnyJson;
    now?: Date;
  }): Promise<MetricValue> {
    const w = params.window ?? ({ type: "lifetime" } as const);
    const filters = params.filters ?? {};
    const now = params.now ?? new Date();

    return await withClient(async (client) => {
      switch (params.metric_key) {
        case "jobs.completed.count":
          return await jobsCompletedCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w, filters);

        case "jobs.completed.split_counts":
          return await jobsCompletedSplitCounts(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "jobs.on_time.count":
          return await jobsOnTimeCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "jobs.on_time.rate_percent":
          return await jobsOnTimeRatePercent(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "jobs.on_time.streak":
          return await jobsOnTimeStreak(client, params.cleaner_id);

        case "jobs.clock_in_out.success.count":
          return await jobsClockInOutSuccessCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "jobs.clock_in_out.missing.count":
          return await jobsClockInOutMissingCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "jobs.photos.valid.count":
          return await jobsPhotosValidCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "jobs.rescheduled.count":
          return await jobsRescheduledCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "jobs.cancelled_by_cleaner.count":
          return await jobsCancelledByCleanerCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "jobs.addons.completed.count":
          return await jobsAddonsCompletedCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "messages.sent_to_clients.meaningful.count":
          return await meaningfulMessagesCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "engagement.meaningful_login_days.count":
          return await meaningfulLoginDaysCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "engagement.login_streak_days":
          return await meaningfulLoginStreakDays(client, params.cleaner_id);

        case "job_requests.accepted.count":
          return await jobRequestsAcceptedCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "job_requests.acceptance_rate_percent":
          return await acceptanceRatePercent(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "clients.repeat_clients.count":
          return await repeatClientsCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w, filters);

        case "clients.count_with_min_jobs":
          return await clientsCountWithMinJobs(client, params.cleaner_id, w, filters);

        case "clients.max_jobs_with_single_client":
          return await clientsMaxJobsSingleClient(client, params.cleaner_id);

        case "ratings.avg_stars":
          return await ratingsAvgStars(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "ratings.five_star.count":
          return await ratingsFiveStarCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "disputes.opened.count":
          return await disputesOpenedCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "disputes.lost.count":
          return await disputesLostCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "disputes.open_or_lost.count":
          return await disputesOpenOrLostCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "disputes.lost.rate_percent_lifetime":
          return await disputesLostRateLifetime(client, params.cleaner_id);

        case "reliability.percentile":
          return await reliabilityPercentile(client, params.cleaner_id);

        case "badges.composite.review_whisperer":
          return await compositeReviewWhisperer(client, params.cleaner_id);

        case "badges.composite.tip_jar_energy":
          return await compositeTipJarEnergy(client, params.cleaner_id);

        case "tips.received.count":
          return await tipsReceivedCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        default:
          return null;
      }
    });
  }
}

/** Window helper (days) */
function windowClause(w: Window, column: string, argIndex: number): { sql: string; args: any[] } {
  if (w.type === "days") {
    return { sql: ` AND ${column} >= now() - ($${argIndex}::int || ' days')::interval`, args: [w.value] };
  }
  return { sql: "", args: [] };
}

/** ---------- Jobs ---------- */

async function jobsCompletedCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window, filters:AnyJson): Promise<number> {
  let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c
             FROM jobs
             WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND status='completed'`;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];

  if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND completed_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }

  if (filters.cleaning_type) { args.push(filters.cleaning_type); sql += ` AND cleaning_type = $${args.length}`; }
  if (filters.time_slot) { args.push(filters.time_slot); sql += ` AND time_slot = $${args.length}`; }

  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
}

async function jobsCompletedSplitCounts(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<Record<string, number>> {
  let sql = `SELECT cleaning_type, count(*)::int AS c
             FROM jobs
             WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND status='completed'`;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND completed_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
  sql += ` GROUP BY cleaning_type`;
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  const out: Record<string, number> = {};
  for (const r of rows) out[r.cleaning_type ?? "unknown"] = r.c;
  return out;
}

async function jobsOnTimeCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  // Uses pt_event_log job.clock_in payload (clock_in_at, scheduled_start_at, lat,lng) + jobs lat/lng
  // On-time = within ±15 min AND within 250m radius at clock-in.
  let sql = `
    WITH clockins AS (
      SELECT
        job_id,
        (payload->>'clock_in_at')::timestamptz AS clock_in_at,
        (payload->>'scheduled_start_at')::timestamptz AS scheduled_start_at,
        (payload->>'lat')::double precision AS lat,
        (payload->>'lng')::double precision AS lng
      FROM pt_event_log
      WHERE event_type='job.clock_in' AND cleaner_id=$1
    )
    SELECT count(*)::int AS c
    FROM jobs j
    JOIN clockins c ON c.job_id=j.id
    WHERE j.cleaner_id=$1 AND j.status='completed'
      AND c.clock_in_at BETWEEN (c.scheduled_start_at - interval '15 minutes') AND (c.scheduled_start_at + interval '15 minutes')
      AND pt_within_radius_meters(c.lat, c.lng, j.lat, j.lng, 250)
  `;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND j.completed_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }

  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
}

async function jobsOnTimeRatePercent(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number|null> {
  // Eligible completed jobs: completed jobs that have a clock_in event with scheduled_start_at and job location.
  let sql = `
    WITH clockins AS (
      SELECT
        job_id,
        (payload->>'clock_in_at')::timestamptz AS clock_in_at,
        (payload->>'scheduled_start_at')::timestamptz AS scheduled_start_at,
        (payload->>'lat')::double precision AS lat,
        (payload->>'lng')::double precision AS lng
      FROM pt_event_log
      WHERE event_type='job.clock_in' AND cleaner_id=$1
    ),
    eligible AS (
      SELECT j.id, j.completed_at,
        CASE WHEN c.clock_in_at BETWEEN (c.scheduled_start_at - interval '15 minutes') AND (c.scheduled_start_at + interval '15 minutes')
              AND pt_within_radius_meters(c.lat, c.lng, j.lat, j.lng, 250)
             THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS on_time
      FROM jobs j
      JOIN clockins c ON c.job_id=j.id
      WHERE j.cleaner_id=$1 AND j.status='completed'
        AND c.clock_in_at IS NOT NULL AND c.scheduled_start_at IS NOT NULL AND j.lat IS NOT NULL AND j.lng IS NOT NULL
    )
    SELECT
      CASE WHEN count(*)=0 THEN NULL
           ELSE round(100.0 * sum(on_time)::numeric / count(*)::numeric, 2)::float END AS pct
    FROM eligible
  `;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` WHERE completed_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  return rows?.[0]?.pct ?? null;
}

async function jobsOnTimeStreak(client:any, cleanerId:string): Promise<number> {
  // Pull last completed jobs ordered desc; stop at first non-on-time.
  const sql = `
    WITH clockins AS (
      SELECT
        job_id,
        (payload->>'clock_in_at')::timestamptz AS clock_in_at,
        (payload->>'scheduled_start_at')::timestamptz AS scheduled_start_at,
        (payload->>'lat')::double precision AS lat,
        (payload->>'lng')::double precision AS lng
      FROM pt_event_log
      WHERE event_type='job.clock_in' AND cleaner_id=$1
    ),
    ordered AS (
      SELECT j.id, j.completed_at,
        CASE WHEN c.clock_in_at BETWEEN (c.scheduled_start_at - interval '15 minutes') AND (c.scheduled_start_at + interval '15 minutes')
              AND pt_within_radius_meters(c.lat, c.lng, j.lat, j.lng, 250)
             THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS on_time
      FROM jobs j
      LEFT JOIN clockins c ON c.job_id=j.id
      WHERE j.cleaner_id=$1 AND j.status='completed'
      ORDER BY j.completed_at DESC
      LIMIT 500
    )
    SELECT count(*)::int AS streak
    FROM (
      SELECT on_time,
             sum(CASE WHEN on_time=0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (ORDER BY completed_at DESC) AS breaks
      FROM ordered
    ) x
    WHERE breaks=0 AND on_time=1
  `;
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, [cleanerId]);
  return rows?.[0]?.streak ?? 0;
}

async function jobsClockInOutSuccessCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  // Both clock_in and clock_out exist and both are within 250m
  let sql = `
    WITH cin AS (
      SELECT job_id,
        (payload->>'lat')::double precision AS lat,
        (payload->>'lng')::double precision AS lng
      FROM pt_event_log
      WHERE event_type='job.clock_in' AND cleaner_id=$1
    ),
    cout AS (
      SELECT job_id,
        (payload->>'lat')::double precision AS lat,
        (payload->>'lng')::double precision AS lng
      FROM pt_event_log
      WHERE event_type='job.clock_out' AND cleaner_id=$1
    )
    SELECT count(*)::int AS c
    FROM jobs j
    JOIN cin i ON i.job_id=j.id
    JOIN cout o ON o.job_id=j.id
    WHERE j.cleaner_id=$1 AND j.status='completed'
      AND pt_within_radius_meters(i.lat, i.lng, j.lat, j.lng, 250)
      AND pt_within_radius_meters(o.lat, o.lng, j.lat, j.lng, 250)
  `;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND j.completed_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
}

async function jobsClockInOutMissingCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  // Completed jobs missing either clock_in or clock_out event
  let sql = `
    WITH cin AS (SELECT DISTINCT job_id FROM pt_event_log WHERE event_type='job.clock_in' AND cleaner_id=$1),
         cout AS (SELECT DISTINCT job_id FROM pt_event_log WHERE event_type='job.clock_out' AND cleaner_id=$1)
    SELECT count(*)::int AS c
    FROM jobs j
    LEFT JOIN cin i ON i.job_id=j.id
    LEFT JOIN cout o ON o.job_id=j.id
    WHERE j.cleaner_id=$1 AND j.status='completed'
      AND (i.job_id IS NULL OR o.job_id IS NULL)
  `;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND j.completed_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
}

async function jobsPhotosValidCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  // >=1 before and >=1 after within [clock_in, clock_out]
  let sql = `
    WITH cin AS (
      SELECT job_id, (payload->>'clock_in_at')::timestamptz AS t_in
      FROM pt_event_log WHERE event_type='job.clock_in' AND cleaner_id=$1
    ),
    cout AS (
      SELECT job_id, (payload->>'clock_out_at')::timestamptz AS t_out
      FROM pt_event_log WHERE event_type='job.clock_out' AND cleaner_id=$1
    ),
    photos AS (
      SELECT job_id,
        (payload->>'phase')::text AS phase,
        (payload->>'taken_at')::timestamptz AS taken_at
      FROM pt_event_log
      WHERE event_type='job.photo_uploaded' AND cleaner_id=$1
    ),
    per_job AS (
      SELECT j.id,
        sum(CASE WHEN p.phase='before' AND p.taken_at BETWEEN i.t_in AND o.t_out THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS before_ok,
        sum(CASE WHEN p.phase='after'  AND p.taken_at BETWEEN i.t_in AND o.t_out THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS after_ok
      FROM jobs j
      JOIN cin i ON i.job_id=j.id
      JOIN cout o ON o.job_id=j.id
      LEFT JOIN photos p ON p.job_id=j.id
      WHERE j.cleaner_id=$1 AND j.status='completed'
      GROUP BY j.id
    )
    SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM per_job WHERE before_ok >= 1 AND after_ok >= 1
  `;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql = sql.replace("WHERE j.cleaner_id=$1 AND j.status='completed'", "WHERE j.cleaner_id=$1 AND j.status='completed' AND j.completed_at >= now() - ($2::int || ' days')::interval"); }
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
}

async function jobsRescheduledCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  // Prefer domain table job_schedule_changes; fallback to event_type='job.rescheduled' if you add it later.
  // If neither exists, returns 0.
  try {
    let sql = `SELECT count(DISTINCT job_id)::int AS c
               FROM job_schedule_changes
               WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND system_initiated=false`;
    const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
    if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND created_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
    const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
    return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
  } catch {
    return 0;
  }
}

async function jobsCancelledByCleanerCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  // Prefer domain table job_cancellations; fallback to event log if exists.
  try {
    let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c
               FROM job_cancellations
               WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND cancellation_initiator='cleaner'`;
    const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
    if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND created_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
    const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
    return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
  } catch {
    // Fallback using event log if you emit job.cancelled events
    const { rows } = await client.query(
      `SELECT count(*)::int AS c
       FROM pt_event_log
       WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND event_type='job.cancelled' AND (payload->>'initiator')='cleaner'`,
      [cleanerId]
    );
    return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
  }
}

async function jobsAddonsCompletedCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  // Prefer event log job.addon_completed. Each line item counts as 1.
  let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c
             FROM pt_event_log
             WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND event_type='job.addon_completed'
               AND (payload->>'completed')::boolean = true`;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND occurred_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
}

/** ---------- Messaging ---------- */

async function meaningfulMessagesCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  // If you already store client_replied_within_24h on messages, use it. Otherwise compute via event log reply within 24h.
  try {
    let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c
      FROM messages
      WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND direction='outbound'
        AND (template_id IS NOT NULL OR text_length >= 25 OR client_replied_within_24h=true)`;
    const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
    if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND created_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
    const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
    return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
  } catch {
    // Event log method: message.sent qualifies if template OR length>=25 OR has message.received in thread within 24h
    let sql = `
      WITH sent AS (
        SELECT
          (payload->>'message_id')::uuid AS message_id,
          (payload->>'thread_id')::uuid AS thread_id,
          (payload->>'template_id')::text AS template_id,
          (payload->>'text_length')::int AS text_length,
          occurred_at
        FROM pt_event_log
        WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND event_type='message.sent'
      ),
      replied AS (
        SELECT
          (payload->>'thread_id')::uuid AS thread_id,
          occurred_at AS replied_at
        FROM pt_event_log
        WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND event_type='message.received'
      ),
      qualified AS (
        SELECT s.*
        FROM sent s
        LEFT JOIN replied r
          ON r.thread_id=s.thread_id AND r.replied_at BETWEEN s.occurred_at AND (s.occurred_at + interval '24 hours')
        WHERE s.template_id IS NOT NULL OR s.text_length >= 25 OR r.thread_id IS NOT NULL
      )
      SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM qualified
    `;
    const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
    if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` WHERE occurred_at >= now() - ($2::int || ' days')::interval`; }
    const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
    return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
  }
}

/** ---------- Engagement ---------- */

async function meaningfulLoginDaysCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  // Distinct local-date days where session started and meaningful action within 15 minutes.
  // We store timezone per session; for simplicity, use timezone from session row (fallback UTC).
  // w.type=days means count days within last N days.
  let sql = `
    WITH sessions AS (
      SELECT session_id, cleaner_id, started_at, COALESCE(timezone, 'UTC') AS tz
      FROM pt_engagement_sessions
      WHERE cleaner_id=$1
    ),
    qualified AS (
      SELECT DISTINCT
        (started_at AT TIME ZONE tz)::date AS local_day
      FROM sessions s
      WHERE EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM pt_engagement_actions a
        WHERE a.session_id=s.session_id
          AND a.occurred_at BETWEEN s.started_at AND (s.started_at + interval '15 minutes')
      )
    )
    SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM qualified
  `;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") {
    args.push(w.value);
    sql = `
      WITH sessions AS (
        SELECT session_id, cleaner_id, started_at, COALESCE(timezone, 'UTC') AS tz
        FROM pt_engagement_sessions
        WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND started_at >= now() - ($2::int || ' days')::interval
      ),
      qualified AS (
        SELECT DISTINCT (started_at AT TIME ZONE tz)::date AS local_day
        FROM sessions s
        WHERE EXISTS (
          SELECT 1 FROM pt_engagement_actions a
          WHERE a.session_id=s.session_id
            AND a.occurred_at BETWEEN s.started_at AND (s.started_at + interval '15 minutes')
        )
      )
      SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM qualified
    `;
  }
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
}

async function meaningfulLoginStreakDays(client:any, cleanerId:string): Promise<number> {
  // Streak computed by checking contiguous local_day values up to the most recent qualified day.
  const sql = `
    WITH sessions AS (
      SELECT session_id, cleaner_id, started_at, COALESCE(timezone,'UTC') AS tz
      FROM pt_engagement_sessions
      WHERE cleaner_id=$1
    ),
    qualified AS (
      SELECT DISTINCT (started_at AT TIME ZONE tz)::date AS local_day
      FROM sessions s
      WHERE EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM pt_engagement_actions a
        WHERE a.session_id=s.session_id
          AND a.occurred_at BETWEEN s.started_at AND (s.started_at + interval '15 minutes')
      )
    ),
    ordered AS (
      SELECT local_day, row_number() OVER (ORDER BY local_day DESC) AS rn
      FROM qualified
      ORDER BY local_day DESC
    ),
    grp AS (
      SELECT local_day, rn,
        (local_day - rn::int) AS g
      FROM ordered
    )
    SELECT count(*)::int AS streak
    FROM grp
    WHERE g = (SELECT g FROM grp ORDER BY local_day DESC LIMIT 1)
  `;
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, [cleanerId]);
  return rows?.[0]?.streak ?? 0;
}

/** ---------- Job Requests / Acceptance ---------- */

async function jobRequestsAcceptedCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  // Uses pt_event_log job_request.decision with payload.decision='accepted'
  let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c
             FROM pt_event_log
             WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND event_type='job_request.decision'
               AND (payload->>'decision')='accepted'`;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND occurred_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
}

async function acceptanceRatePercent(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number|null> {
  // acceptance = accepts / (accepts + declines_non_good_faith)
  // good-faith reasons are included only up to 6 per 7 days; after that they count as non-good-faith.
  // classifier uses payload.good_faith_reason if present; the app should set it based on rules (distance>=11mi, out of availability, mismatch, safety, access, short_notice<18h).
  // Enforcement of limits performed here by counting good-faith declines in rolling 7 days at event time.
  // For simplicity, we compute for a window (days) by looking at events in that window, but limit is evaluated in rolling 7 days within that same window.
  let sql = `
    WITH decisions AS (
      SELECT occurred_at,
             (payload->>'decision') AS decision,
             (payload->>'good_faith_reason') AS gfr
      FROM pt_event_log
      WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND event_type='job_request.decision'
  `;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND occurred_at >= now() - ($2::int || ' days')::interval`; }
  sql += `
    ),
    tagged AS (
      SELECT d.*,
        CASE WHEN decision='declined' AND gfr IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_good_faith_decline
      FROM decisions d
    ),
    limited AS (
      SELECT t.*,
        CASE
          WHEN t.is_good_faith_decline=1 THEN
            CASE
              WHEN (
                SELECT count(*) FROM tagged t2
                WHERE t2.is_good_faith_decline=1
                  AND t2.occurred_at BETWEEN (t.occurred_at - interval '7 days') AND t.occurred_at
              ) <= 6
              THEN 1 ELSE 0
            END
          ELSE 0
        END AS good_faith_exempt
      FROM tagged t
    ),
    agg AS (
      SELECT
        sum(CASE WHEN decision='accepted' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS accepts,
        sum(CASE WHEN decision='declined' AND NOT (good_faith_exempt=1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS declines_non_good_faith
      FROM limited
    )
    SELECT CASE
      WHEN (accepts + declines_non_good_faith)=0 THEN NULL
      ELSE round(100.0 * accepts::numeric / (accepts + declines_non_good_faith)::numeric, 2)::float
    END AS pct
    FROM agg
  `;
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  return rows?.[0]?.pct ?? null;
}

/** ---------- Clients / Loyalty ---------- */

async function repeatClientsCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window, filters:AnyJson): Promise<number> {
  const minJobs = Number(filters.min_completed_jobs_per_client ?? 2);
  let sql = `
    SELECT count(*)::int AS c
    FROM (
      SELECT client_id, count(*) AS jobs
      FROM jobs
      WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND status='completed'
  `;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND completed_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
  sql += ` GROUP BY client_id
    ) x
    WHERE x.jobs >= $${args.length+1}
  `;
  args.push(minJobs);
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
}

async function clientsCountWithMinJobs(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window, filters:AnyJson): Promise<number> {
  const minJobs = Number(filters.min_completed_jobs_per_client ?? 3);
  return await repeatClientsCount(client, cleanerId, w, { min_completed_jobs_per_client: minJobs });
}

async function clientsMaxJobsSingleClient(client:any, cleanerId:string): Promise<number> {
  const sql = `
    SELECT COALESCE(max(cnt),0)::int AS m
    FROM (
      SELECT client_id, count(*)::int AS cnt
      FROM jobs
      WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND status='completed'
      GROUP BY client_id
    ) x
  `;
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, [cleanerId]);
  return rows?.[0]?.m ?? 0;
}

/** ---------- Ratings ---------- */

async function ratingsAvgStars(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number|null> {
  let sql = `SELECT avg(stars)::float AS a FROM ratings WHERE cleaner_id=$1`;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND created_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  const a = rows?.[0]?.a;
  return a == null ? null : Number(a);
}

async function ratingsFiveStarCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM ratings WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND stars=5`;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND created_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
}

/** ---------- Disputes ---------- */

async function disputesOpenedCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  try {
    let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM disputes WHERE cleaner_id=$1`;
    const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
    if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND opened_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
    const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
    return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
  } catch {
    let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM pt_event_log WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND event_type='dispute.opened'`;
    const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
    if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND occurred_at >= now() - ($2::int || ' days')::interval`; }
    const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
    return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
  }
}

async function disputesLostCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  try {
    let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM disputes WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND outcome='lost'`;
    const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
    if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND resolved_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
    const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
    return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
  } catch {
    let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c
               FROM pt_event_log
               WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND event_type='dispute.resolved' AND (payload->>'outcome')='lost'`;
    const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
    if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND occurred_at >= now() - ($2::int || ' days')::interval`; }
    const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
    return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
  }
}

async function disputesOpenOrLostCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  try {
    let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c
               FROM disputes
               WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND status IN ('open','lost')`;
    const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
    if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND opened_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
    const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
    return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
  } catch {
    // Event-based approximation: opened minus resolved-won/dismissed isn't perfect; default to lost in window + opened in window.
    const opened = await disputesOpenedCount(client, cleanerId, w);
    const lost = await disputesLostCount(client, cleanerId, w);
    return opened + lost;
  }
}

async function disputesLostRateLifetime(client:any, cleanerId:string): Promise<number|null> {
  const lost = await disputesLostCount(client, cleanerId, { type:"lifetime" });
  const completed = await jobsCompletedCount(client, cleanerId, { type:"lifetime" }, {});
  if (!completed) return null;
  return Math.round((100.0 * lost / completed) * 100) / 100;
}

/** ---------- Reliability Percentile ---------- */

async function reliabilityPercentile(client:any, cleanerId:string): Promise<number|null> {
  // Expect reliability_scores table: cleaner_id, reliability_score, region_id, computed_at
  try {
    const sql = `
      WITH active AS (
        SELECT cleaner_id, reliability_score
        FROM reliability_scores
        WHERE computed_at >= now() - interval '30 days'
      ),
      ranked AS (
        SELECT cleaner_id,
               percent_rank() OVER (ORDER BY reliability_score DESC) AS pr
        FROM active
      )
      SELECT round((pr * 100.0)::numeric, 2)::float AS pct
      FROM ranked
      WHERE cleaner_id=$1
    `;
    const { rows } = await client.query(sql, [cleanerId]);
    return rows?.[0]?.pct ?? null;
  } catch {
    return null;
  }
}

/** ---------- Composite badges ---------- */

async function compositeReviewWhisperer(client:any, cleanerId:string): Promise<boolean> {
  // template 'tmpl_review_request' used >=15 AND ratings count >=5 in last 60 days
  const sql = `
    WITH tmpl AS (
      SELECT count(*)::int AS c
      FROM pt_event_log
      WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND event_type='message.sent'
        AND (payload->>'template_id')='tmpl_review_request'
        AND occurred_at >= now() - interval '60 days'
    ),
    rev AS (
      SELECT count(*)::int AS c
      FROM ratings
      WHERE cleaner_id=$1
        AND created_at >= now() - interval '60 days'
    )
    SELECT (tmpl.c >= 15 AND rev.c >= 5) AS ok
    FROM tmpl, rev
  `;
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, [cleanerId]);
  return Boolean(rows?.[0]?.ok);
}

async function compositeTipJarEnergy(client:any, cleanerId:string): Promise<boolean> {
  // template 'tmpl_tip_request' used >=10 AND tips >=3 in last 90 days
  const sql = `
    WITH tmpl AS (
      SELECT count(*)::int AS c
      FROM pt_event_log
      WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND event_type='message.sent'
        AND (payload->>'template_id')='tmpl_tip_request'
        AND occurred_at >= now() - interval '90 days'
    ),
    t AS (
      SELECT count(*)::int AS c
      FROM tips
      WHERE cleaner_id=$1
        AND created_at >= now() - interval '90 days'
    )
    SELECT (tmpl.c >= 10 AND t.c >= 3) AS ok
    FROM tmpl, t
  `;
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, [cleanerId]);
  return Boolean(rows?.[0]?.ok);
}

async function tipsReceivedCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  try {
    let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM tips WHERE cleaner_id=$1`;
    const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
    if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND created_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
    const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
    return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
  } catch {
    let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM pt_event_log WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND event_type='tip.received'`;
    const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
    if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND occurred_at >= now() - ($2::int || ' days')::interval`; }
    const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
    return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
  }
}
""".strip()+"\n"

(services/"sql_metric_provider_full.ts").write_text(provider)

# Verification harness: seed script template and expected outputs format
verify_md = f"""# Step 7 — Metric Verification Harness

This step ships a **complete SqlMetricProvider** plus SQL helpers.

## 1) Run SQL helpers
Execute:
- `sql/step7_sql_helpers.sql` (adds `pt_haversine_meters`, `pt_haversine_miles`, `pt_within_radius_meters`)

## 2) Add the provider
Replace the scaffold provider with:
- `src/services/sql_metric_provider_full.ts`

## 3) Verify metrics with a deterministic seed
Create a seed dataset for a single cleaner `CLEANER_ID` and run each metric query through the provider.
Recommended approach:
- Seed rows in: `jobs`, `pt_event_log`, `pt_engagement_sessions`, `pt_engagement_actions`, `ratings`, `disputes`, `messages`, `tips`
- Then call the provider for each key.

### Minimum seed you should include
- 3 completed jobs (basic/deep/moveout), 1 weekend, 1 early_morning
- clock_in + clock_out events for each, inside 250m
- at least one clock_in outside ±15 minutes (to validate on-time false)
- before/after photo events between punches for 2 jobs, missing for 1 job
- 10 message.sent events with a template and 10 with long text
- 2 message.received replies within 24h
- 6 good-faith declines and 1 extra (to validate the cap)
- 5 ratings with 3 five-stars
- 1 dispute opened, 1 dispute lost, 1 dispute won
- 3 add-on completion events
- 5 meaningful sessions with actions within 15 minutes

## 4) Coverage checklist
This module implements these metrics (count: {len(metric_keys)}):
{chr(10).join([f"- `{k}`" for k in metric_keys])}

## Notes
- `jobs.rescheduled.count` and `jobs.cancelled_by_cleaner.count` will use domain tables if present; otherwise fall back (or return 0).
- If you want *everything* event-backed, add new events: `job.rescheduled`, `job.cancelled`.
"""
( base/"VERIFY_STEP7.md").write_text(verify_md)

Analysis paused
import json, zipfile
from pathlib import Path

base = Path("/mnt/data/puretask_gamification_step7")
base.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
(src:=base/"src").mkdir(exist_ok=True)
(services:=src/"services").mkdir(exist_ok=True)
(sql_dir:=base/"sql").mkdir(exist_ok=True)
(tests:=base/"tests").mkdir(exist_ok=True)

metrics = json.loads(Path("/mnt/data/metrics_contract_v1.json").read_text())["metrics"]
metric_keys = [m["key"] for m in metrics]

sql_helpers = """
-- Step 7 SQL helpers for Metrics Contract v1
BEGIN;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION pt_haversine_meters(lat1 double precision, lon1 double precision, lat2 double precision, lon2 double precision)
RETURNS double precision
LANGUAGE sql
IMMUTABLE
AS $$
  SELECT 2 * 6371000 * asin(
    sqrt(
      pow(sin(radians((lat2 - lat1) / 2)), 2) +
      cos(radians(lat1)) * cos(radians(lat2)) * pow(sin(radians((lon2 - lon1) / 2)), 2)
    )
  );
$$;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION pt_haversine_miles(lat1 double precision, lon1 double precision, lat2 double precision, lon2 double precision)
RETURNS double precision
LANGUAGE sql
IMMUTABLE
AS $$
  SELECT pt_haversine_meters(lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2) / 1609.344;
$$;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION pt_within_radius_meters(lat1 double precision, lon1 double precision, lat2 double precision, lon2 double precision, radius_m double precision)
RETURNS boolean
LANGUAGE sql
IMMUTABLE
AS $$
  SELECT pt_haversine_meters(lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2) <= radius_m;
$$;

COMMIT;
""".strip()+"\n"
(sql_dir/"step7_sql_helpers.sql").write_text(sql_helpers)

provider = Path("/mnt/data/puretask_gamification_step7_provider.ts")
provider.write_text("""
import { MetricProvider, MetricValue, Window } from "../../puretask_gamification_step5/src/types";
import { withClient } from "../db/client";

/**
 * SqlMetricProvider (FULL, v1)
 * Implements every metric in metrics_contract_v1.json.
 *
 * IMPORTANT ASSUMPTIONS (sane defaults):
 * - Domain tables exist: jobs, ratings, disputes, messages, tips, job_requests, cleaner_level_status, cleaner_settings.
 * - Event log exists: pt_event_log, pt_engagement_sessions, pt_engagement_actions (from Step 4).
 * - Jobs table contains: id, cleaner_id, client_id, status, completed_at, cleaning_type, time_slot, lat, lng
 * - Scheduled start stored on job.clock_in payload as scheduled_start_at (authoritative for on-time).
 */

type AnyJson = Record<string, any>;

export class SqlMetricProvider implements MetricProvider {
  async getMetric(params: {
    cleaner_id: string;
    metric_key: string;
    window?: Window | null;
    filters?: AnyJson;
    now?: Date;
  }): Promise<MetricValue> {
    const w = params.window ?? ({ type: "lifetime" } as const);
    const filters = params.filters ?? {};

    return await withClient(async (client) => {
      switch (params.metric_key) {
        case "jobs.completed.count":
          return await jobsCompletedCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w, filters);

        case "jobs.completed.split_counts":
          return await jobsCompletedSplitCounts(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "jobs.on_time.count":
          return await jobsOnTimeCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "jobs.on_time.rate_percent":
          return await jobsOnTimeRatePercent(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "jobs.on_time.streak":
          return await jobsOnTimeStreak(client, params.cleaner_id);

        case "jobs.clock_in_out.success.count":
          return await jobsClockInOutSuccessCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "jobs.clock_in_out.missing.count":
          return await jobsClockInOutMissingCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "jobs.photos.valid.count":
          return await jobsPhotosValidCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "jobs.rescheduled.count":
          return await jobsRescheduledCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "jobs.cancelled_by_cleaner.count":
          return await jobsCancelledByCleanerCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "jobs.addons.completed.count":
          return await jobsAddonsCompletedCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "messages.sent_to_clients.meaningful.count":
          return await meaningfulMessagesCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "engagement.meaningful_login_days.count":
          return await meaningfulLoginDaysCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "engagement.login_streak_days":
          return await meaningfulLoginStreakDays(client, params.cleaner_id);

        case "job_requests.accepted.count":
          return await jobRequestsAcceptedCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "job_requests.acceptance_rate_percent":
          return await acceptanceRatePercent(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "clients.repeat_clients.count":
          return await repeatClientsCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w, filters);

        case "clients.count_with_min_jobs":
          return await clientsCountWithMinJobs(client, params.cleaner_id, w, filters);

        case "clients.max_jobs_with_single_client":
          return await clientsMaxJobsSingleClient(client, params.cleaner_id);

        case "ratings.avg_stars":
          return await ratingsAvgStars(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "ratings.five_star.count":
          return await ratingsFiveStarCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "disputes.opened.count":
          return await disputesOpenedCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "disputes.lost.count":
          return await disputesLostCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "disputes.open_or_lost.count":
          return await disputesOpenOrLostCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        case "disputes.lost.rate_percent_lifetime":
          return await disputesLostRateLifetime(client, params.cleaner_id);

        case "reliability.percentile":
          return await reliabilityPercentile(client, params.cleaner_id);

        case "badges.composite.review_whisperer":
          return await compositeReviewWhisperer(client, params.cleaner_id);

        case "badges.composite.tip_jar_energy":
          return await compositeTipJarEnergy(client, params.cleaner_id);

        case "tips.received.count":
          return await tipsReceivedCount(client, params.cleaner_id, w);

        default:
          return null;
      }
    });
  }
}

/** ---------- Jobs ---------- */

async function jobsCompletedCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window, filters:AnyJson): Promise<number> {
  let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM jobs WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND status='completed'`;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND completed_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
  if (filters.cleaning_type) { args.push(filters.cleaning_type); sql += ` AND cleaning_type=$${args.length}`; }
  if (filters.time_slot) { args.push(filters.time_slot); sql += ` AND time_slot=$${args.length}`; }
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
}

async function jobsCompletedSplitCounts(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<Record<string, number>> {
  let sql = `SELECT cleaning_type, count(*)::int AS c FROM jobs WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND status='completed'`;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND completed_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
  sql += ` GROUP BY cleaning_type`;
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  const out: Record<string, number> = {};
  for (const r of rows) out[r.cleaning_type ?? "unknown"] = r.c;
  return out;
}

async function jobsOnTimeCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  let sql = `
    WITH clockins AS (
      SELECT job_id,
        (payload->>'clock_in_at')::timestamptz AS clock_in_at,
        (payload->>'scheduled_start_at')::timestamptz AS scheduled_start_at,
        (payload->>'lat')::double precision AS lat,
        (payload->>'lng')::double precision AS lng
      FROM pt_event_log
      WHERE event_type='job.clock_in' AND cleaner_id=$1
    )
    SELECT count(*)::int AS c
    FROM jobs j
    JOIN clockins c ON c.job_id=j.id
    WHERE j.cleaner_id=$1 AND j.status='completed'
      AND c.clock_in_at BETWEEN (c.scheduled_start_at - interval '15 minutes') AND (c.scheduled_start_at + interval '15 minutes')
      AND pt_within_radius_meters(c.lat, c.lng, j.lat, j.lng, 250)
  `;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND j.completed_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
}

async function jobsOnTimeRatePercent(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number|null> {
  let sql = `
    WITH clockins AS (
      SELECT job_id,
        (payload->>'clock_in_at')::timestamptz AS clock_in_at,
        (payload->>'scheduled_start_at')::timestamptz AS scheduled_start_at,
        (payload->>'lat')::double precision AS lat,
        (payload->>'lng')::double precision AS lng
      FROM pt_event_log
      WHERE event_type='job.clock_in' AND cleaner_id=$1
    ),
    eligible AS (
      SELECT j.id, j.completed_at,
        CASE WHEN c.clock_in_at BETWEEN (c.scheduled_start_at - interval '15 minutes') AND (c.scheduled_start_at + interval '15 minutes')
              AND pt_within_radius_meters(c.lat, c.lng, j.lat, j.lng, 250)
             THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS on_time
      FROM jobs j
      JOIN clockins c ON c.job_id=j.id
      WHERE j.cleaner_id=$1 AND j.status='completed'
    )
    SELECT CASE WHEN count(*)=0 THEN NULL
                ELSE round(100.0 * sum(on_time)::numeric / count(*)::numeric, 2)::float END AS pct
    FROM eligible
  `;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql = sql.replace("FROM eligible", f"FROM eligible WHERE completed_at >= now() - ($2::int || ' days')::interval"); }
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  return rows?.[0]?.pct ?? null;
}

async function jobsOnTimeStreak(client:any, cleanerId:string): Promise<number> {
  const sql = `
    WITH clockins AS (
      SELECT job_id,
        (payload->>'clock_in_at')::timestamptz AS clock_in_at,
        (payload->>'scheduled_start_at')::timestamptz AS scheduled_start_at,
        (payload->>'lat')::double precision AS lat,
        (payload->>'lng')::double precision AS lng
      FROM pt_event_log
      WHERE event_type='job.clock_in' AND cleaner_id=$1
    ),
    ordered AS (
      SELECT j.id, j.completed_at,
        CASE WHEN c.clock_in_at BETWEEN (c.scheduled_start_at - interval '15 minutes') AND (c.scheduled_start_at + interval '15 minutes')
              AND pt_within_radius_meters(c.lat, c.lng, j.lat, j.lng, 250)
             THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS on_time
      FROM jobs j
      LEFT JOIN clockins c ON c.job_id=j.id
      WHERE j.cleaner_id=$1 AND j.status='completed'
      ORDER BY j.completed_at DESC
      LIMIT 500
    ),
    marks AS (
      SELECT on_time, completed_at,
             sum(CASE WHEN on_time=0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (ORDER BY completed_at DESC) AS breaks
      FROM ordered
    )
    SELECT count(*)::int AS streak
    FROM marks
    WHERE breaks=0 AND on_time=1
  `;
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, [cleanerId]);
  return rows?.[0]?.streak ?? 0;
}

async function jobsClockInOutSuccessCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  let sql = `
    WITH cin AS (
      SELECT job_id,
        (payload->>'lat')::double precision AS lat,
        (payload->>'lng')::double precision AS lng
      FROM pt_event_log
      WHERE event_type='job.clock_in' AND cleaner_id=$1
    ),
    cout AS (
      SELECT job_id,
        (payload->>'lat')::double precision AS lat,
        (payload->>'lng')::double precision AS lng
      FROM pt_event_log
      WHERE event_type='job.clock_out' AND cleaner_id=$1
    )
    SELECT count(*)::int AS c
    FROM jobs j
    JOIN cin i ON i.job_id=j.id
    JOIN cout o ON o.job_id=j.id
    WHERE j.cleaner_id=$1 AND j.status='completed'
      AND pt_within_radius_meters(i.lat, i.lng, j.lat, j.lng, 250)
      AND pt_within_radius_meters(o.lat, o.lng, j.lat, j.lng, 250)
  `;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND j.completed_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
}

async function jobsClockInOutMissingCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  let sql = `
    WITH cin AS (SELECT DISTINCT job_id FROM pt_event_log WHERE event_type='job.clock_in' AND cleaner_id=$1),
         cout AS (SELECT DISTINCT job_id FROM pt_event_log WHERE event_type='job.clock_out' AND cleaner_id=$1)
    SELECT count(*)::int AS c
    FROM jobs j
    LEFT JOIN cin i ON i.job_id=j.id
    LEFT JOIN cout o ON o.job_id=j.id
    WHERE j.cleaner_id=$1 AND j.status='completed'
      AND (i.job_id IS NULL OR o.job_id IS NULL)
  `;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND j.completed_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
}

async function jobsPhotosValidCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  let args:any[] = [cleanerId]
  let days_clause = ""
  if (w.type=="days"):
    args.append(w.value)
    days_clause = " AND j.completed_at >= now() - ($2::int || ' days')::interval"
  const sql = f"""
    WITH cin AS (
      SELECT job_id, (payload->>'clock_in_at')::timestamptz AS t_in
      FROM pt_event_log WHERE event_type='job.clock_in' AND cleaner_id=$1
    ),
    cout AS (
      SELECT job_id, (payload->>'clock_out_at')::timestamptz AS t_out
      FROM pt_event_log WHERE event_type='job.clock_out' AND cleaner_id=$1
    ),
    photos AS (
      SELECT job_id, (payload->>'phase')::text AS phase, (payload->>'taken_at')::timestamptz AS taken_at
      FROM pt_event_log WHERE event_type='job.photo_uploaded' AND cleaner_id=$1
    ),
    per_job AS (
      SELECT j.id,
        sum(CASE WHEN p.phase='before' AND p.taken_at BETWEEN i.t_in AND o.t_out THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS before_ok,
        sum(CASE WHEN p.phase='after'  AND p.taken_at BETWEEN i.t_in AND o.t_out THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS after_ok
      FROM jobs j
      JOIN cin i ON i.job_id=j.id
      JOIN cout o ON o.job_id=j.id
      LEFT JOIN photos p ON p.job_id=j.id
      WHERE j.cleaner_id=$1 AND j.status='completed'{days_clause}
      GROUP BY j.id
    )
    SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM per_job WHERE before_ok >= 1 AND after_ok >= 1
  """
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
}

async function jobsRescheduledCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  try {
    let sql = `SELECT count(DISTINCT job_id)::int AS c FROM job_schedule_changes WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND system_initiated=false`;
    const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
    if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND created_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
    const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
    return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
  } catch {
    return 0;
  }
}

async function jobsCancelledByCleanerCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  try {
    let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM job_cancellations WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND cancellation_initiator='cleaner'`;
    const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
    if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND created_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
    const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
    return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
  } catch {
    const { rows } = await client.query(
      `SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM pt_event_log WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND event_type='job.cancelled' AND (payload->>'initiator')='cleaner'`,
      [cleanerId]
    );
    return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
  }
}

async function jobsAddonsCompletedCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c
             FROM pt_event_log
             WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND event_type='job.addon_completed'
               AND (payload->>'completed')::boolean = true`;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND occurred_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
}

/** ---------- Messaging ---------- */

async function meaningfulMessagesCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  try {
    let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c
      FROM messages
      WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND direction='outbound'
        AND (template_id IS NOT NULL OR text_length >= 25 OR client_replied_within_24h=true)`;
    const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
    if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND created_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
    const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
    return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
  } catch {
    let sql = `
      WITH sent AS (
        SELECT (payload->>'thread_id')::uuid AS thread_id,
               (payload->>'template_id')::text AS template_id,
               (payload->>'text_length')::int AS text_length,
               occurred_at
        FROM pt_event_log
        WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND event_type='message.sent'
      ),
      replied AS (
        SELECT (payload->>'thread_id')::uuid AS thread_id,
               occurred_at AS replied_at
        FROM pt_event_log
        WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND event_type='message.received'
      )
      SELECT count(*)::int AS c
      FROM sent s
      LEFT JOIN replied r
        ON r.thread_id=s.thread_id AND r.replied_at BETWEEN s.occurred_at AND (s.occurred_at + interval '24 hours')
      WHERE s.template_id IS NOT NULL OR s.text_length >= 25 OR r.thread_id IS NOT NULL
    `;
    const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
    if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND s.occurred_at >= now() - ($2::int || ' days')::interval`; }
    const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
    return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
  }
}

/** ---------- Engagement ---------- */

async function meaningfulLoginDaysCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  let sql = `
    WITH sessions AS (
      SELECT session_id, started_at, COALESCE(timezone,'UTC') AS tz
      FROM pt_engagement_sessions
      WHERE cleaner_id=$1
    ),
    qualified AS (
      SELECT DISTINCT (started_at AT TIME ZONE tz)::date AS local_day
      FROM sessions s
      WHERE EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM pt_engagement_actions a
        WHERE a.session_id=s.session_id
          AND a.occurred_at BETWEEN s.started_at AND (s.started_at + interval '15 minutes')
      )
    )
    SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM qualified
  `;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") {
    args.push(w.value);
    sql = `
      WITH sessions AS (
        SELECT session_id, started_at, COALESCE(timezone,'UTC') AS tz
        FROM pt_engagement_sessions
        WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND started_at >= now() - ($2::int || ' days')::interval
      ),
      qualified AS (
        SELECT DISTINCT (started_at AT TIME ZONE tz)::date AS local_day
        FROM sessions s
        WHERE EXISTS (
          SELECT 1 FROM pt_engagement_actions a
          WHERE a.session_id=s.session_id
            AND a.occurred_at BETWEEN s.started_at AND (s.started_at + interval '15 minutes')
        )
      )
      SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM qualified
    `;
  }
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
}

async function meaningfulLoginStreakDays(client:any, cleanerId:string): Promise<number> {
  const sql = `
    WITH sessions AS (
      SELECT session_id, started_at, COALESCE(timezone,'UTC') AS tz
      FROM pt_engagement_sessions
      WHERE cleaner_id=$1
    ),
    qualified AS (
      SELECT DISTINCT (started_at AT TIME ZONE tz)::date AS local_day
      FROM sessions s
      WHERE EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM pt_engagement_actions a
        WHERE a.session_id=s.session_id
          AND a.occurred_at BETWEEN s.started_at AND (s.started_at + interval '15 minutes')
      )
    ),
    ordered AS (
      SELECT local_day, row_number() OVER (ORDER BY local_day DESC) AS rn
      FROM qualified
      ORDER BY local_day DESC
    ),
    grp AS (
      SELECT local_day, (local_day - rn::int) AS g FROM ordered
    )
    SELECT count(*)::int AS streak
    FROM grp
    WHERE g = (SELECT g FROM grp ORDER BY local_day DESC LIMIT 1)
  `;
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, [cleanerId]);
  return rows?.[0]?.streak ?? 0;
}

/** ---------- Acceptance ---------- */

async function jobRequestsAcceptedCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM pt_event_log
             WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND event_type='job_request.decision'
               AND (payload->>'decision')='accepted'`;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND occurred_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
}

async function acceptanceRatePercent(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number|null> {
  let sql = `
    WITH decisions AS (
      SELECT occurred_at, (payload->>'decision') AS decision, (payload->>'good_faith_reason') AS gfr
      FROM pt_event_log
      WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND event_type='job_request.decision'
  `;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND occurred_at >= now() - ($2::int || ' days')::interval`; }
  sql += `
    ),
    tagged AS (
      SELECT d.*, CASE WHEN decision='declined' AND gfr IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_gf
      FROM decisions d
    ),
    limited AS (
      SELECT t.*,
        CASE
          WHEN t.is_gf=1 THEN
            CASE WHEN (
              SELECT count(*) FROM tagged t2
              WHERE t2.is_gf=1
                AND t2.occurred_at BETWEEN (t.occurred_at - interval '7 days') AND t.occurred_at
            ) <= 6 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
          ELSE 0
        END AS gf_exempt
      FROM tagged t
    ),
    agg AS (
      SELECT
        sum(CASE WHEN decision='accepted' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS accepts,
        sum(CASE WHEN decision='declined' AND gf_exempt=0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS declines_non_gf
      FROM limited
    )
    SELECT CASE WHEN (accepts + declines_non_gf)=0 THEN NULL
                ELSE round(100.0 * accepts::numeric / (accepts + declines_non_gf)::numeric, 2)::float END AS pct
    FROM agg
  `;
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  return rows?.[0]?.pct ?? null;
}

/** ---------- Clients ---------- */

async function repeatClientsCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window, filters:AnyJson): Promise<number> {
  const minJobs = Number(filters.min_completed_jobs_per_client ?? 2);
  let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM (
               SELECT client_id, count(*) AS jobs
               FROM jobs
               WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND status='completed'`;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND completed_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
  sql += ` GROUP BY client_id
           ) x WHERE x.jobs >= $${args.length+1}`;
  args.push(minJobs);
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
}

async function clientsCountWithMinJobs(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window, filters:AnyJson): Promise<number> {
  const minJobs = Number(filters.min_completed_jobs_per_client ?? 3);
  return await repeatClientsCount(client, cleanerId, w, { min_completed_jobs_per_client: minJobs });
}

async function clientsMaxJobsSingleClient(client:any, cleanerId:string): Promise<number> {
  const sql = `
    SELECT COALESCE(max(cnt),0)::int AS m
    FROM (SELECT client_id, count(*)::int AS cnt
          FROM jobs WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND status='completed'
          GROUP BY client_id) x
  `;
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, [cleanerId]);
  return rows?.[0]?.m ?? 0;
}

/** ---------- Ratings ---------- */

async function ratingsAvgStars(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number|null> {
  let sql = `SELECT avg(stars)::float AS a FROM ratings WHERE cleaner_id=$1`;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND created_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  const a = rows?.[0]?.a;
  return a == null ? null : Number(a);
}

async function ratingsFiveStarCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM ratings WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND stars=5`;
  const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
  if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND created_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
  return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
}

/** ---------- Disputes ---------- */

async function disputesOpenedCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  try {
    let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM disputes WHERE cleaner_id=$1`;
    const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
    if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND opened_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
    const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
    return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
  } catch {
    let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM pt_event_log WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND event_type='dispute.opened'`;
    const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
    if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND occurred_at >= now() - ($2::int || ' days')::interval`; }
    const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
    return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
  }
}

async function disputesLostCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  try {
    let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM disputes WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND outcome='lost'`;
    const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
    if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND resolved_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
    const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
    return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
  } catch {
    let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM pt_event_log WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND event_type='dispute.resolved' AND (payload->>'outcome')='lost'`;
    const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
    if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND occurred_at >= now() - ($2::int || ' days')::interval`; }
    const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
    return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
  }
}

async function disputesOpenOrLostCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  try {
    let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM disputes WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND status IN ('open','lost')`;
    const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
    if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND opened_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
    const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
    return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
  } catch {
    const opened = await disputesOpenedCount(client, cleanerId, w);
    const lost = await disputesLostCount(client, cleanerId, w);
    return opened + lost;
  }
}

async function disputesLostRateLifetime(client:any, cleanerId:string): Promise<number|null> {
  const lost = await disputesLostCount(client, cleanerId, { type:"lifetime" } as any);
  const completed = await jobsCompletedCount(client, cleanerId, { type:"lifetime" } as any, {});
  if (!completed) return null;
  return Math.round((100.0 * lost / completed) * 100) / 100;
}

/** ---------- Reliability ---------- */

async function reliabilityPercentile(client:any, cleanerId:string): Promise<number|null> {
  try {
    const sql = `
      WITH active AS (
        SELECT cleaner_id, reliability_score
        FROM reliability_scores
        WHERE computed_at >= now() - interval '30 days'
      ),
      ranked AS (
        SELECT cleaner_id,
               percent_rank() OVER (ORDER BY reliability_score DESC) AS pr
        FROM active
      )
      SELECT round((pr * 100.0)::numeric, 2)::float AS pct
      FROM ranked
      WHERE cleaner_id=$1
    `;
    const { rows } = await client.query(sql, [cleanerId]);
    return rows?.[0]?.pct ?? null;
  } catch {
    return null;
  }
}

/** ---------- Composite badges ---------- */

async function compositeReviewWhisperer(client:any, cleanerId:string): Promise<boolean> {
  const sql = `
    WITH tmpl AS (
      SELECT count(*)::int AS c
      FROM pt_event_log
      WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND event_type='message.sent'
        AND (payload->>'template_id')='tmpl_review_request'
        AND occurred_at >= now() - interval '60 days'
    ),
    rev AS (
      SELECT count(*)::int AS c
      FROM ratings
      WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND created_at >= now() - interval '60 days'
    )
    SELECT (tmpl.c >= 15 AND rev.c >= 5) AS ok FROM tmpl, rev
  `;
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, [cleanerId]);
  return Boolean(rows?.[0]?.ok);
}

async function compositeTipJarEnergy(client:any, cleanerId:string): Promise<boolean> {
  const sql = `
    WITH tmpl AS (
      SELECT count(*)::int AS c
      FROM pt_event_log
      WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND event_type='message.sent'
        AND (payload->>'template_id')='tmpl_tip_request'
        AND occurred_at >= now() - interval '90 days'
    ),
    t AS (
      SELECT count(*)::int AS c
      FROM tips
      WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND created_at >= now() - interval '90 days'
    )
    SELECT (tmpl.c >= 10 AND t.c >= 3) AS ok FROM tmpl, t
  `;
  const { rows } = await client.query(sql, [cleanerId]);
  return Boolean(rows?.[0]?.ok);
}

async function tipsReceivedCount(client:any, cleanerId:string, w:Window): Promise<number> {
  try {
    let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM tips WHERE cleaner_id=$1`;
    const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
    if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND created_at >= now() - ($${args.length}::int || ' days')::interval`; }
    const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
    return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
  } catch {
    let sql = `SELECT count(*)::int AS c FROM pt_event_log WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND event_type='tip.received'`;
    const args:any[] = [cleanerId];
    if (w.type === "days") { args.push(w.value); sql += ` AND occurred_at >= now() - ($2::int || ' days')::interval`; }
    const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
    return rows?.[0]?.c ?? 0;
  }
}

Step 7.1 — Run SQL Helpers (distance + radius)

-- Step 7 SQL helpers for Metrics Contract v1
BEGIN;

-- Haversine distance in meters
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION pt_haversine_meters(lat1 double precision, lon1 double precision, lat2 double precision, lon2 double precision)
RETURNS double precision
LANGUAGE sql
IMMUTABLE
AS $$
  SELECT 2 * 6371000 * asin(
    sqrt(
      pow(sin(radians((lat2 - lat1) / 2)), 2) +
      cos(radians(lat1)) * cos(radians(lat2)) * pow(sin(radians((lon2 - lon1) / 2)), 2)
    )
  );
$$;

-- Haversine distance in miles
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION pt_haversine_miles(lat1 double precision, lon1 double precision, lat2 double precision, lon2 double precision)
RETURNS double precision
LANGUAGE sql
IMMUTABLE
AS $$
  SELECT pt_haversine_meters(lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2) / 1609.344;
$$;

-- Within radius (meters)
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION pt_within_radius_meters(lat1 double precision, lon1 double precision, lat2 double precision, lon2 double precision, radius_m double precision)
RETURNS boolean
LANGUAGE sql
IMMUTABLE
AS $$
  SELECT pt_haversine_meters(lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2) <= radius_m;
$$;

COMMIT;
This enables:

✅ GPS radius checks (250m)

✅ “distance too far” (≥11 miles) good-faith decline validation (if you choose to validate server-side too)

✅ future ranking guardrails

Step 7.2 — Replace the scaffold with the FULL SqlMetricProvider
