# Badge awarding service
(services/"badge_service.ts").write_text("""import { withClient } from "../db/client";

/**
 * BadgeService
 * - loads enabled badge_definitions
 * - evaluates triggers against provided metric snapshot
 * - awards badges idempotently
 * - writes to cleaner_achievement_feed (optional)
 *
 * Integration:
 * - call after metric recompute for a cleaner (worker)
 */
export type MetricSnapshot = Record<string, number | string | boolean | null>;

type Trigger =
  | { type: "metric"; metric: string; op: ">=" | "==" | ">" | "<=" | "<"; target: number }
  | { type: "window"; window: { type: "days" | "last_jobs"; n: number }; conditions: Array<{ metric: string; op: ">=" | "==" | ">" | "<=" | "<"; target: number }> }
  | { type: "compound"; all: Trigger[] }
  | { type: "derived"; derivation: string; op: ">=" | "==" | ">" | "<=" | "<"; target: number };

export class BadgeService {
  async awardEligibleBadges(params: {
    cleaner_id: string;
    metric_snapshot: MetricSnapshot;
  }): Promise<{ newly_awarded: string[] }> {
    const { cleaner_id, metric_snapshot } = params;
    const defs = await this.getEnabledBadgeDefinitions();

    const newly: string[] = [];
    for (const d of defs) {
      const already = await this.hasBadge(cleaner_id, d.id);
      if (already) continue;

      const trig = d.trigger as Trigger;
      if (this.evaluateTrigger(trig, metric_snapshot)) {
        const ok = await this.awardBadge(cleaner_id, d.id, { trigger: trig });
        if (ok) newly.push(d.id);
      }
    }

    return { newly_awarded: newly };
  }

  private evaluateTrigger(t: Trigger, m: MetricSnapshot): boolean {
    switch (t.type) {
      case "metric":
        return this.compare(Number(m[t.metric] ?? 0), t.op, t.target);
      case "window":
        // Window metrics are pre-aggregated in snapshot by the engine (Metrics Contract).
        // e.g. jobs.completed.window, disputes.opened.window
        return t.conditions.every(c => this.compare(Number(m[c.metric] ?? 0), c.op, c.target));
      case "compound":
        return t.all.every(x => this.evaluateTrigger(x as any, m));
      case "derived":
        return this.compare(Number(m[t.derivation] ?? 0), t.op, t.target);
      default:
        return false;
    }
  }

  private compare(val: number, op: string, target: number): boolean {
    if (op === ">=") return val >= target;
    if (op === "==") return val === target;
    if (op === ">") return val > target;
    if (op === "<=") return val <= target;
    if (op === "<") return val < target;
    return false;
  }

  private async getEnabledBadgeDefinitions(): Promise<any[]> {
    return await withClient(async (client) => {
      const { rows } = await client.query(
        `SELECT id, name, description, category, icon_key, is_profile_visible, sort_order, trigger
         FROM badge_definitions
         WHERE is_enabled=true
         ORDER BY category ASC, sort_order ASC`
      );
      return rows ?? [];
    });
  }

  private async hasBadge(cleaner_id: string, badge_id: string): Promise<boolean> {
    return await withClient(async (client) => {
      const { rowCount } = await client.query(
        `SELECT 1 FROM cleaner_badges WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND badge_id=$2 LIMIT 1`,
        [cleaner_id, badge_id]
      );
      return rowCount > 0;
    });
  }

  private async awardBadge(cleaner_id: string, badge_id: string, meta: any): Promise<boolean> {
    return await withClient(async (client) => {
      await client.query("BEGIN");
      try {
        const ins = await client.query(
          `INSERT INTO cleaner_badges (cleaner_id, badge_id, meta)
           VALUES ($1,$2,$3::jsonb)
           ON CONFLICT (cleaner_id, badge_id) DO NOTHING
           RETURNING id`,
          [cleaner_id, badge_id, JSON.stringify(meta ?? {})]
        );
        if (ins.rowCount === 0) {
          await client.query("ROLLBACK");
          return false;
        }

        // Feed entry
        const def = await client.query(
          `SELECT name, description FROM badge_definitions WHERE id=$1`,
          [badge_id]
        );
        const title = def.rows?.[0]?.name ?? "Badge earned";
        const subtitle = def.rows?.[0]?.description ?? null;

        await client.query(
          `INSERT INTO cleaner_achievement_feed (cleaner_id, type, ref_id, title, subtitle, meta)
           VALUES ($1,'badge',$2,$3,$4,$5::jsonb)`,
          [cleaner_id, badge_id, title, subtitle, JSON.stringify({ badge_id })]
        );

        await client.query("COMMIT");
        return true;
      } catch (e) {
        await client.query("ROLLBACK");
        throw e;
      }
    });
  }
}
""")

# Routes for badge gallery and feed
(routes/"badges.ts").write_text("""import { Router } from "express";
import { withClient } from "../db/client";

export const badgesRouter = Router();

/**
 * Badge catalog (enabled)
 */
badgesRouter.get("/badges", async (req, res) => {
  const rows = await withClient(async (client) => {
    const { rows } = await client.query(
      `SELECT id, name, description, category, icon_key, is_profile_visible, sort_order
       FROM badge_definitions
       WHERE is_enabled=true
       ORDER BY category ASC, sort_order ASC`
    );
    return rows ?? [];
  });
  res.json({ ok: true, badges: rows });
});

/**
 * Cleaner badges (earned)
 */
badgesRouter.get("/cleaners/:id/badges", async (req, res) => {
  const cleaner_id = req.params.id;
  const rows = await withClient(async (client) => {
    const { rows } = await client.query(
      `SELECT cb.badge_id, cb.earned_at, bd.name, bd.description, bd.category, bd.icon_key, bd.is_profile_visible
       FROM cleaner_badges cb
       JOIN badge_definitions bd ON bd.id=cb.badge_id
       WHERE cb.cleaner_id=$1
       ORDER BY cb.earned_at DESC`,
      [cleaner_id]
    );
    return rows ?? [];
  });
  res.json({ ok: true, earned: rows });
});

/**
 * Achievement feed (recent)
 */
badgesRouter.get("/cleaners/:id/achievements", async (req, res) => {
  const cleaner_id = req.params.id;
  const limit = Math.min(Number(req.query.limit ?? 50), 200);
  const rows = await withClient(async (client) => {
    const { rows } = await client.query(
      `SELECT type, ref_id, title, subtitle, meta, created_at
       FROM cleaner_achievement_feed
       WHERE cleaner_id=$1
       ORDER BY created_at DESC
       LIMIT $2`,
      [cleaner_id, limit]
    );
    return rows ?? [];
  });
  res.json({ ok: true, feed: rows });
});
""")

# Admin endpoints to upsert badge defs (optional) - uses config versions pattern; keep minimal: enable/disable + sort/profile visibility
(routes/"admin_badges.ts").write_text("""import { Router } from "express";
import { requireAdminRole } from "../middleware/rbac";
import { withClient } from "../db/client";

export const adminBadgesRouter = Router();

adminBadgesRouter.post("/admin/badges/upsert", requireAdminRole("ops"), async (req, res) => {
  const b = req.body ?? {};
  const id = String(b.id);
  if (!id) return res.status(400).json({ ok:false, error:"id required" });

  const row = await withClient(async (client) => {
    const { rows } = await client.query(
      `INSERT INTO badge_definitions
        (id, name, description, category, icon_key, is_profile_visible, is_enabled, sort_order, trigger, updated_at)
       VALUES ($1,$2,$3,$4,$5,$6,$7,$8,$9::jsonb, now())
       ON CONFLICT (id)
       DO UPDATE SET name=EXCLUDED.name,
                     description=EXCLUDED.description,
                     category=EXCLUDED.category,
                     icon_key=EXCLUDED.icon_key,
                     is_profile_visible=EXCLUDED.is_profile_visible,
                     is_enabled=EXCLUDED.is_enabled,
                     sort_order=EXCLUDED.sort_order,
                     trigger=EXCLUDED.trigger,
                     updated_at=now()
       RETURNING *`,
      [
        id,
        String(b.name ?? ""),
        String(b.description ?? ""),
        String(b.category ?? "fun"),
        b.icon_key ?? null,
        Boolean(b.is_profile_visible ?? false),
        Boolean(b.is_enabled ?? true),
        Number(b.sort_order ?? 0),
        JSON.stringify(b.trigger ?? {})
      ]
    );
    return rows?.[0] ?? null;
  });

  res.json({ ok:true, badge: row });
});

adminBadgesRouter.post("/admin/badges/enable", requireAdminRole("ops"), async (req, res) => {
  const { id, is_enabled } = req.body ?? {};
  const row = await withClient(async (client) => {
    const { rows } = await client.query(
      `UPDATE badge_definitions SET is_enabled=$2, updated_at=now() WHERE id=$1 RETURNING id, is_enabled`,
      [String(id), Boolean(is_enabled)]
    );
    return rows?.[0] ?? null;
  });
  res.json({ ok:true, badge: row });
});
""")

# Docs for integration with worker (Step 6 pipeline)
(docs/"integration.md").write_text("""# Step 14 — Badge system integration

## 1) Seed badge_definitions
Run migration, then insert badge definitions from config/badge_definitions_v1.json.

Example seed:
- for each badge JSON object:
  INSERT INTO badge_definitions (...)

(You can also POST /admin/badges/upsert for each.)

## 2) Award badges from worker
After you recompute a cleaner's metric snapshot (Step 6) and persist:
- call BadgeService.awardEligibleBadges({ cleaner_id, metric_snapshot })

`metric_snapshot` should include metric keys referenced by badge triggers, including:
- lifetime counters (jobs.completed.lifetime, ratings.five_star.lifetime, etc.)
- window metrics (jobs.completed.window, disputes.opened.window, etc.)
- derived metrics (streaks) if you implement them in Metrics Contract

## 3) UI endpoints
- GET /badges (catalog)
- GET /cleaners/:id/badges (earned)
- GET /cleaners/:id/achievements (feed)

## 4) Achievement feed
Badge awards write to cleaner_achievement_feed automatically.
You can also write:
- level_up
- goal_complete
- reward_granted
to the same feed for a unified “Recent Activity” UX.
""")

# README
(base/"README.md").write_text("""# Step 14 — Badge System (core + fun) + Achievement Feed

## Includes
- Postgres migration: badge_definitions, cleaner_badges, cleaner_achievement_feed
- Badge definitions JSON (lean core + fun personality badges)
- BadgeService: evaluates triggers and awards idempotently + writes feed entries
- API routes for badge catalog, cleaner earned badges, achievement feed
- Optional admin routes to upsert/enable badges

## Integration
1) Run sql/step14_badges_migration_v1_0.sql
2) Seed badge_definitions from config/badge_definitions_v1.json
3) Call BadgeService after metric recompute in worker
4) Mount badgesRouter and adminBadgesRouter
""")

# zip
zip_path = Path("/mnt/data/puretask_gamification_step14_badges.zip")
with zipfile.ZipFile(zip_path, "w", zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED) as z:
    for p in base.rglob("*"):
        if p.is_file():
            z.write(p, arcname=str(p.relative_to(base.parent)))
zip_path.as_posix()
Result
'/mnt/data/puretask_gamification_step14_badges.zip'
✅ Step 14 COMPLETE: Badge System (Core + Fun) + Achievement Feed + APIs

This step implements the full badge layer exactly how we locked it:

lean “core” badges aligned to goals

fun/personality badges that still map to real behavior

idempotent awarding (earn once)

unified achievement feed so UI can show “Recent achievements”

admin ability to enable/disable and tune badges

Download
puretask_gamification_step14_badges.zip

What’s inside Step 14
1) Postgres migration
sql/step14_badges_migration_v1_0.sql
Creates:

badge_definitions (trigger stored as JSON)

cleaner_badges (earned badges, idempotent via unique constraint)

cleaner_achievement_feed (badge/level_up/goal/reward activity stream)

2) BadgeDefinitions JSON (locked set)
config/badge_definitions_v1.json
Includes:

Core badges (First Job, 5-Star, Repeat Client, Dispute-Free, Job Milestones, Deep/Move-out/Add-on specialist, Weekend/Early)

Fun/personality badges (Booked & Busy, Back-to-Back, The Closer, Rapid Responder, Good Vibes Only, Thank-You Machine, Review Whisperer, Tip Jar Energy, Clockwork, No Drama, Smooth Operator, Steady Hands, Add-On Animal, Photo Proof Pro, Safety First)

3) BadgeService (award engine)
src/services/badge_service.ts

loads enabled badge definitions

evaluates triggers against a metric snapshot

awards badge idempotently

writes an entry to cleaner_achievement_feed

4) API routes for app UI
src/routes/badges.ts
Endpoints:

GET /badges (catalog)

GET /cleaners/:id/badges (earned badges)

GET /cleaners/:id/achievements (recent feed)

5) Optional admin endpoints
src/routes/admin_badges.ts

POST /admin/badges/upsert (create/update definition)

POST /admin/badges/enable (toggle)

(Uses RBAC middleware from Step 10.)

6) Integration doc
docs/integration.md
Explains exactly how to:

seed definitions

award badges after metric recompute (Step 6)

use the feed for UI

Integration checklist
Run migration:

sql/step14_badges_migration_v1_0.sql

Seed badge_definitions:

insert from config/badge_definitions_v1.json

or call POST /admin/badges/upsert per badge

In your worker (after metric recompute + persist):

call:

BadgeService.awardEligibleBadges({ cleaner_id, metric_snapshot })

Mount routes:

app.use(badgesRouter);
app.use(adminBadgesRouter); // behind admin auth
Next step in our outline: ✅ Step 15 — Seasonal Challenges Engine (multipliers + time windows + admin config + safe application)
We’ve built:

goals ✅

rewards ✅

effects + ranking ✅

admin control plane ✅

runtime config loader ✅

cash budget enforcement ✅

badges ✅

Next is Seasonal Challenges (no resets):

season_rules config (start/end + multipliers)

apply multipliers safely during metric aggregation

admin can schedule & toggle events

UI can show “active season” and progress boosts

Reply “continue” and I’ll generate Step 15 as a zip (migrations + season engine + integration patches + admin endpoints). 


lets continue

            except: pass

# Migration for season rules + applications table
(sql_dir/"step15_seasonal_challenges_migration_v1_0.sql").write_text("""-- Step 15 — Seasonal Challenges Engine (v1.0)
-- Time-bounded multipliers + eligibility + region targeting + auditability.

BEGIN;

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS season_rules (
  id text PRIMARY KEY,
  name text NOT NULL,
  description text NOT NULL,
  starts_at timestamptz NOT NULL,
  ends_at timestamptz NOT NULL,
  is_enabled boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
  -- region targeting: null/empty = global
  regions text[] NULL,
  -- multiplier rules stored as JSON
  -- example:
  -- { "multipliers": [ { "metric_prefix":"jobs.weekend.", "multiplier": 2.0 } ],
  --   "goal_overrides":[ { "goal_id":"lvl4_weekend_core", "multiplier":2.0 } ],
  --   "ui": { "banner_copy":"Weekend Warrior Month" } }
  rule jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
  created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_season_rules_active_time
  ON season_rules (is_enabled, starts_at, ends_at);

-- Optional: record when a multiplier was applied (for debugging/analytics)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS season_application_log (
  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  season_id text NOT NULL REFERENCES season_rules(id),
  cleaner_id uuid NOT NULL,
  region_id text NULL,
  applied_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  context jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_season_app_cleaner_time
  ON season_application_log (cleaner_id, applied_at DESC);

COMMIT;
""")

# Season service: load active seasons for region/time; apply to metric snapshot in memory
(services/"season_service.ts").write_text("""import { withClient } from "../db/client";

export type SeasonRule = {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  description: string;
  starts_at: string;
  ends_at: string;
  is_enabled: boolean;
  regions: string[] | null;
  rule: any;
};

export class SeasonService {
  async getActiveSeasons(params: { region_id?: string | null; at?: Date }): Promise<SeasonRule[]> {
    const region_id = params.region_id ?? null;
    const at = params.at ?? new Date();

    return await withClient(async (client) => {
      const { rows } = await client.query(
        `SELECT id, name, description, starts_at, ends_at, is_enabled, regions, rule
         FROM season_rules
         WHERE is_enabled=true
           AND starts_at <= $1::timestamptz
           AND ends_at >= $1::timestamptz
           AND (
             regions IS NULL OR array_length(regions,1) IS NULL OR $2::text IS NULL OR $2 = ANY(regions)
           )
         ORDER BY starts_at ASC`,
        [at.toISOString(), region_id]
      );
      return (rows ?? []).map((r:any) => ({
        ...r,
        starts_at: new Date(r.starts_at).toISOString(),
        ends_at: new Date(r.ends_at).toISOString()
      }));
    });
  }

  /**
   * Apply seasonal multipliers to a metric snapshot.
   * This does NOT mutate stored truth; it only affects progress counting.
   *
   * Expected season.rule format:
   * { multipliers: [ { metric_key, multiplier } ], ui: {...} }
   */
  applyMultipliers(params: {
    metric_snapshot: Record<string, any>;
    seasons: SeasonRule[];
  }): { snapshot: Record<string, any>; applied: Array<{ season_id: string; metric_key: string; multiplier: number }> } {
    const original = params.metric_snapshot ?? {};
    const snapshot: Record<string, any> = { ...original };
    const applied: Array<{ season_id: string; metric_key: string; multiplier: number }> = [];

    for (const s of params.seasons ?? []) {
      const mults = s.rule?.multipliers ?? [];
      for (const m of mults) {
        const key = String(m.metric_key ?? "");
        const factor = Number(m.multiplier ?? 1.0);
        if (!key || !isFinite(factor) || factor === 1.0) continue;

        const base = Number(snapshot[key] ?? 0);
        // Multipliers apply only to numeric counters
        if (!isFinite(base)) continue;

        snapshot[key] = base * factor;
        applied.push({ season_id: s.id, metric_key: key, multiplier: factor });
      }
    }
    return { snapshot, applied };
  }
}
""")

# Integration patch: where to apply in evaluation pipeline
(docs/"integration.md").write_text("""# Step 15 — Seasonal Challenges Integration

## Where seasons apply
Seasonal multipliers should affect *progress counting* without changing base truth metrics.

Recommended flow in worker:
1) Build base metric_snapshot (from events / aggregates)
2) Load active seasons for cleaner region:
   `seasons = await seasonService.getActiveSeasons({ region_id })`
3) Apply multipliers:
   `seasoned = seasonService.applyMultipliers({ metric_snapshot, seasons })`
4) Evaluate goals/levels using `seasoned.snapshot`
5) Persist:
   - base metric_snapshot as truth (optional)
   - store seasoned.applied metadata in evaluation result (optional)
6) UI:
   Show active seasons + banner copy + "boost applied" chips

## Anti-gaming & fairness
- Multipliers never guarantee jobs.
- Multipliers affect only goal progress.
- Season rules are time-bounded (starts_at/ends_at).
- Regions can be targeted by setting season_rules.regions.

## Logging (optional)
Record season_application_log rows when:
- multipliers applied AND at least one progress delta occurs
""")

# API routes: list active seasons (for app UI), admin CRUD (minimal)
(routes/"seasons.ts").write_text("""import { Router } from "express";
import { SeasonService } from "../services/season_service";
import { withClient } from "../db/client";
import { requireAdminRole } from "../middleware/rbac";

export const seasonsRouter = Router();
const seasonSvc = new SeasonService();

// Public/app endpoint: active seasons for region
seasonsRouter.get("/seasons/active", async (req, res) => {
  const region_id = (req.query.region_id as string) ?? null;
  const seasons = await seasonSvc.getActiveSeasons({ region_id, at: new Date() });
  // Return minimal UI data
  res.json({
    ok: true,
    seasons: seasons.map(s => ({
      id: s.id,
      name: s.name,
      description: s.description,
      starts_at: s.starts_at,
      ends_at: s.ends_at,
      ui: s.rule?.ui ?? {}
    }))
  });
});

// Admin: list all seasons
seasonsRouter.get("/admin/seasons", requireAdminRole("support"), async (req, res) => {
  const rows = await withClient(async (client) => {
    const { rows } = await client.query(
      `SELECT id, name, description, starts_at, ends_at, is_enabled, regions, rule
       FROM season_rules
       ORDER BY starts_at DESC`
    );
    return rows ?? [];
  });
  res.json({ ok:true, seasons: rows });
});

// Admin: upsert season rule
seasonsRouter.post("/admin/seasons/upsert", requireAdminRole("ops"), async (req, res) => {
  const s = req.body ?? {};
  const id = String(s.id);
  if (!id) return res.status(400).json({ ok:false, error:"id required" });

  const row = await withClient(async (client) => {
    const { rows } = await client.query(
      `INSERT INTO season_rules (id, name, description, starts_at, ends_at, is_enabled, regions, rule, updated_at)
       VALUES ($1,$2,$3,$4::timestamptz,$5::timestamptz,$6,$7,$8::jsonb, now())
       ON CONFLICT (id)
       DO UPDATE SET name=EXCLUDED.name,
                     description=EXCLUDED.description,
                     starts_at=EXCLUDED.starts_at,
                     ends_at=EXCLUDED.ends_at,
                     is_enabled=EXCLUDED.is_enabled,
                     regions=EXCLUDED.regions,
                     rule=EXCLUDED.rule,
                     updated_at=now()
       RETURNING *`,
      [
        id,
        String(s.name ?? ""),
        String(s.description ?? ""),
        String(s.starts_at),
        String(s.ends_at),
        Boolean(s.is_enabled ?? true),
        (s.regions ?? null),
        JSON.stringify(s.rule ?? {})
      ]
    );
    return rows?.[0] ?? null;
  });

  res.json({ ok:true, season: row });
});

// Admin: enable/disable
seasonsRouter.post("/admin/seasons/enable", requireAdminRole("ops"), async (req, res) => {
  const { id, is_enabled } = req.body ?? {};
  const row = await withClient(async (client) => {
    const { rows } = await client.query(
      `UPDATE season_rules SET is_enabled=$2, updated_at=now() WHERE id=$1 RETURNING id, is_enabled`,
      [String(id), Boolean(is_enabled)]
    );
    return rows?.[0] ?? null;
  });
  res.json({ ok:true, season: row });
});
""")

# README
(base/"README.md").write_text("""# Step 15 — Seasonal Challenges Engine (multipliers + scheduling + admin controls)

## Includes
- DB migration: season_rules + season_application_log
- SeasonService:
  - getActiveSeasons(region_id, at)
  - applyMultipliers(metric_snapshot, seasons)
- Default seasonal rules JSON (Spring, Move-Out, Holiday, End-of-Year, Weekend Warrior)
- API routes:
  - GET /seasons/active?region_id=...
  - Admin CRUD: /admin/seasons*

## Integration
1) Run sql/step15_seasonal_challenges_migration_v1_0.sql
2) Seed season_rules from config/season_rules_v1.json
3) In worker evaluation pipeline:
   - load active seasons for cleaner region
   - apply multipliers to metric_snapshot BEFORE evaluating goals
4) Mount seasonsRouter (and ensure RBAC middleware is available for admin routes)
""")

  name text NOT NULL,
  description text NOT NULL,

  is_enabled boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
  -- region targeting: null/empty = global

  -- { "multipliers": [ { "metric_key":"jobs.weekend.completed.count", "multiplier": 2.0 } ],

);

  ON season_rules (is_enabled, starts_at, ends_at);

(services/"season_service.ts").write_text("""import { withClient } from "../db/client";

export type SeasonRule = {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  description: string;
  starts_at: string;
  ends_at: string;
  is_enabled: boolean;
  regions: string[] | null;
  rule: any;
};

export class SeasonService {
  async getActiveSeasons(params: { region_id?: string | null; at?: Date }): Promise<SeasonRule[]> {
    const region_id = params.region_id ?? null;
    const at = params.at ?? new Date();

    return await withClient(async (client) => {
      const { rows } = await client.query(
        `SELECT id, name, description, starts_at, ends_at, is_enabled, regions, rule
         FROM season_rules
         WHERE is_enabled=true
           AND starts_at <= $1::timestamptz
           AND ends_at >= $1::timestamptz
           AND (
             regions IS NULL OR array_length(regions,1) IS NULL OR $2::text IS NULL OR $2 = ANY(regions)
           )
         ORDER BY starts_at ASC`,
        [at.toISOString(), region_id]
      );
      return (rows ?? []).map((r:any) => ({
        ...r,
        starts_at: new Date(r.starts_at).toISOString(),
        ends_at: new Date(r.ends_at).toISOString()
      }));
    });
  }

  /**
   * Apply seasonal multipliers to a metric snapshot.
   * This does NOT mutate stored truth; it only affects progress counting.
   *
   * Expected season.rule format:
   * { multipliers: [ { metric_key, multiplier } ], ui: {...} }
   */
  applyMultipliers(params: {
    metric_snapshot: Record<string, any>;
    seasons: SeasonRule[];
  }): { snapshot: Record<string, any>; applied: Array<{ season_id: string; metric_key: string; multiplier: number }> } {
    const original = params.metric_snapshot ?? {};
    const snapshot: Record<string, any> = { ...original };
    const applied: Array<{ season_id: string; metric_key: string; multiplier: number }> = [];

    for (const s of params.seasons ?? []) {
      const mults = s.rule?.multipliers ?? [];
      for (const m of mults) {
        const key = String(m.metric_key ?? "");
        const factor = Number(m.multiplier ?? 1.0);
        if (!key || !isFinite(factor) || factor === 1.0) continue;

        const base = Number(snapshot[key] ?? 0);
        if (!isFinite(base)) continue;

        snapshot[key] = base * factor;
        applied.push({ season_id: s.id, metric_key: key, multiplier: factor });
      }
    }
    return { snapshot, applied };
  }
}
""")

(routes/"seasons.ts").write_text("""import { Router } from "express";
import { SeasonService } from "../services/season_service";
import { withClient } from "../db/client";
import { requireAdminRole } from "../middleware/rbac";

export const seasonsRouter = Router();
const seasonSvc = new SeasonService();

// App endpoint: active seasons for region
seasonsRouter.get("/seasons/active", async (req, res) => {
  const region_id = (req.query.region_id as string) ?? null;
  const seasons = await seasonSvc.getActiveSeasons({ region_id, at: new Date() });
  res.json({
    ok: true,
    seasons: seasons.map(s => ({
      id: s.id,
      name: s.name,
      description: s.description,
      starts_at: s.starts_at,
      ends_at: s.ends_at,
      ui: s.rule?.ui ?? {}
    }))
  });
});

// Admin: list all
seasonsRouter.get("/admin/seasons", requireAdminRole("support"), async (req, res) => {
  const rows = await withClient(async (client) => {
    const { rows } = await client.query(
      `SELECT id, name, description, starts_at, ends_at, is_enabled, regions, rule
       FROM season_rules
       ORDER BY starts_at DESC`
    );
    return rows ?? [];
  });
  res.json({ ok:true, seasons: rows });
});

// Admin: upsert
seasonsRouter.post("/admin/seasons/upsert", requireAdminRole("ops"), async (req, res) => {
  const s = req.body ?? {};
  const id = String(s.id);
  if (!id) return res.status(400).json({ ok:false, error:"id required" });

  const row = await withClient(async (client) => {
    const { rows } = await client.query(
      `INSERT INTO season_rules (id, name, description, starts_at, ends_at, is_enabled, regions, rule, updated_at)
       VALUES ($1,$2,$3,$4::timestamptz,$5::timestamptz,$6,$7,$8::jsonb, now())
       ON CONFLICT (id)
       DO UPDATE SET name=EXCLUDED.name,
                     description=EXCLUDED.description,
                     starts_at=EXCLUDED.starts_at,
                     ends_at=EXCLUDED.ends_at,
                     is_enabled=EXCLUDED.is_enabled,
                     regions=EXCLUDED.regions,
                     rule=EXCLUDED.rule,
                     updated_at=now()
       RETURNING *`,
      [
        id,
        String(s.name ?? ""),
        String(s.description ?? ""),
        String(s.starts_at),
        String(s.ends_at),
        Boolean(s.is_enabled ?? true),
        (s.regions ?? null),
        JSON.stringify(s.rule ?? {})
      ]
    );
    return rows?.[0] ?? null;
  });

  res.json({ ok:true, season: row });
});

// Admin: enable/disable
seasonsRouter.post("/admin/seasons/enable", requireAdminRole("ops"), async (req, res) => {
  const { id, is_enabled } = req.body ?? {};
  const row = await withClient(async (client) => {
    const { rows } = await client.query(
      `UPDATE season_rules SET is_enabled=$2, updated_at=now() WHERE id=$1 RETURNING id, is_enabled`,
      [String(id), Boolean(is_enabled)]
    );
    return rows?.[0] ?? null;
  });
  res.json({ ok:true, season: row });
});
""")

(docs/"integration.md").write_text("""# Step 15 — Seasonal Challenges Integration

## Where seasons apply
Seasonal multipliers affect *progress counting* without changing base truth metrics.

Recommended worker flow:
1) Build base metric_snapshot (truth)
2) Load active seasons for cleaner region:
   `seasons = await seasonService.getActiveSeasons({ region_id })`
3) Apply multipliers:
   `seasoned = seasonService.applyMultipliers({ metric_snapshot, seasons })`
4) Evaluate goals/levels using `seasoned.snapshot`
5) Persist:
   - base metric snapshot (truth) as you already do
   - optionally attach `seasoned.applied` to evaluation result for UI/debug

## UI
- Call `GET /seasons/active?region_id=...`
- Show banner + "boost applied" chips for affected metrics

## Optional logging
Write season_application_log when:
- multipliers applied AND at least one progress delta occurs
""")

# clean

# Service: NextBestAction
(services/"next_best_action_service.ts").write_text("""import { withClient } from "../db/client";
import { ConfigProvider } from "./config_provider";
import { SeasonService } from "./season_service";

/**
 * Step 16 — Next Best Action + Reward Preview
 *
 * Purpose:
 * - Give cleaners 1–3 concrete actions that move them closest to next reward/level
 * - Show what reward unlocks (cash or non-cash) + duration
 * - Be fair: respect pause status, region caps, active seasons
 *
 * Inputs:
 * - Cleaner metric snapshot (truth), optionally season-multiplied for progress display
 * - Goal definitions from ConfigProvider (DB configs)
 * - Goal progress from cleaner_goal_progress (if you persist it), else computed deltas
 *
 * Output:
 * - next_best_actions[] ordered by "time-to-reward" heuristic
 */

type AnyJson = Record<string, any>;

export type NextBestAction = {
  goal_id: string;
  level: number;
  goal_type: "core" | "stretch" | "maintenance";
  title: string;
  description: string;
  metric_key: string;
  target: number;
  current: number;
  remaining: number;
  percent_complete: number;
  reward_preview: Array<{
    reward_id: string;
    kind: string;
    label: string;
    duration_days?: number | null;
    amount_cents?: number | null;
    uses?: number | null;
  }>;
  earnings_impact_hint?: string | null;
  season_boosts?: Array<{ season_id: string; metric_key: string; multiplier: number }>;
};

export class NextBestActionService {
  constructor(
    private configProvider: ConfigProvider,
    private seasonService: SeasonService
  ) {}

  async getNextBestActions(params: {
    cleaner_id: string;
    region_id?: string | null;
    limit?: number;
  }): Promise<{ paused: boolean; pause_reasons: string[]; actions: NextBestAction[] }> {
    const cleaner_id = params.cleaner_id;
    const region_id = params.region_id ?? null;
    const limit = Math.min(Number(params.limit ?? 3), 10);

    const pause = await this.getPauseStatus(cleaner_id);
    if (pause.paused) {
      return { paused: true, pause_reasons: pause.pause_reasons, actions: [] };
    }

    const bundle = await this.configProvider.getBundle(region_id);
    const goals: AnyJson[] = (bundle.goals?.goals ?? bundle.goals ?? []) as AnyJson[];
    const rewards: AnyJson[] = (bundle.rewards?.rewards ?? bundle.rewards ?? []) as AnyJson[];

    const rewardsById = new Map<string, AnyJson>();
    for (const r of rewards) rewardsById.set(r.id, r);

    // metric snapshot (truth) from DB if you store it; else infer from aggregates tables.
    const metricSnapshot = await this.getMetricSnapshot(cleaner_id);

    // Apply seasonal multipliers ONLY for display/progress evaluation (not for truth storage).
    const seasons = await this.seasonService.getActiveSeasons({ region_id, at: new Date() });
    const applied = this.seasonService.applyMultipliers({ metric_snapshot: metricSnapshot, seasons });

    const completedGoalIds = await this.getCompletedGoalIds(cleaner_id);

    // Candidate goals: incomplete core/stretches first; maintenance is not an "action" unless failing.
    const candidates = goals
      .filter(g => g && g.id && !completedGoalIds.has(String(g.id)))
      .filter(g => ["core","stretch","maintenance"].includes(String(g.type ?? g.goal_type ?? "core")))
      .map(g => this.toAction(g, rewardsById, applied.snapshot, applied.applied));

    // Rank heuristic:
    // 1) core goals first
    // 2) smallest remaining
    // 3) higher level slightly preferred if tie (motivates)
    // 4) higher reward value if tie
    const ranked = candidates.sort((a,b) => {
      const typeWeight = (t: string) => (t === "core" ? 0 : t === "stretch" ? 1 : 2);
      const tw = typeWeight(a.goal_type) - typeWeight(b.goal_type);
      if (tw !== 0) return tw;

      if (a.remaining !== b.remaining) return a.remaining - b.remaining;

      if (a.level !== b.level) return b.level - a.level;

      const val = (x: NextBestAction) =>
        x.reward_preview.reduce((sum, r) => sum + (r.amount_cents ?? 0), 0);
      return val(b) - val(a);
    });

    return { paused: false, pause_reasons: [], actions: ranked.slice(0, limit) };
  }

  private toAction(goal: AnyJson, rewardsById: Map<string, AnyJson>, snapshot: AnyJson, season_boosts: any[]): NextBestAction {
    const level = Number(goal.level ?? goal.level_number ?? 1);
    const goal_type = String(goal.type ?? goal.goal_type ?? "core") as any;
    const metric_key = String(goal.metric_key ?? goal.metric?.key ?? goal.metric ?? "");
    const target = Number(goal.target ?? goal.metric?.target ?? 0);

    const current = Number(snapshot[metric_key] ?? 0);
    const remaining = Math.max(target - current, 0);
    const percent = target > 0 ? Math.min((current / target) * 100, 100) : (remaining === 0 ? 100 : 0);

    const rewardIds: string[] = goal.reward_ids ?? [];
    const reward_preview = rewardIds.map((rid) => {
      const r = rewardsById.get(rid) ?? {};
      const kind = String(r.kind ?? "unknown");
      const label = this.rewardLabel(r);
      return {
        reward_id: rid,
        kind,
        label,
        duration_days: r.duration_days ?? null,
        amount_cents: r.params?.amount_cents ?? null,
        uses: r.uses ?? r.params?.uses ?? null
      };
    });

    const earnings_impact_hint = this.earningsHint(goal, reward_preview);

    const boostsForMetric = (season_boosts ?? []).filter((x:any) => String(x.metric_key) === metric_key)
      .map((x:any) => ({ season_id: x.season_id, metric_key: x.metric_key, multiplier: x.multiplier }));

    return {
      goal_id: String(goal.id),
      level,
      goal_type,
      title: String(goal.title ?? goal.name ?? "Goal"),
      description: String(goal.description ?? ""),
      metric_key,
      target,
      current,
      remaining,
      percent_complete: Math.round(percent*10)/10,
      reward_preview,
      earnings_impact_hint,
      season_boosts: boostsForMetric.length ? boostsForMetric : undefined
    };
  }

  private rewardLabel(r: AnyJson): string {
    const kind = String(r.kind ?? "");
    if (kind === "cash_bonus") {
      const cents = Number(r.params?.amount_cents ?? 0);
      return cents > 0 ? `$${(cents/100).toFixed(0)} cash bonus` : "Cash bonus";
    }
    if (kind === "visibility_multiplier") {
      const m = Number(r.params?.multiplier ?? 1.0);
      const d = r.duration_days ? ` for ${r.duration_days} days` : "";
      return `Visibility boost x${m}${d}`;
    }
    if (kind === "early_exposure_minutes") {
      const mins = Number(r.params?.minutes ?? 0);
      const d = r.duration_days ? ` for ${r.duration_days} days` : "";
      return `Early exposure +${mins} min${d}`;
    }
    if (kind === "platform_fee_discount_percent") {
      const p = Number(r.params?.percent ?? 0);
      const d = r.duration_days ? ` for ${r.duration_days} days` : "";
      return `Platform fee -${p}%${d}`;
    }
    if (kind === "instant_payout_fee_waiver") {
      const uses = Number(r.params?.uses ?? 1);
      return `Instant payout fee waived (${uses} uses)`;
    }
    return String(r.label ?? r.name ?? kind || "Reward");
  }

  private earningsHint(goal: AnyJson, preview: Array<any>): string | null {
    // Lightweight, non-financial "hint" copy; not a promise.
    // We avoid hard $ estimates unless you have historical conversion models.
    const hasVisibility = preview.some(p => p.kind === "visibility_multiplier" || p.kind === "early_exposure_minutes");
    const hasFeeDiscount = preview.some(p => p.kind === "platform_fee_discount_percent");
    const hasCash = preview.some(p => p.kind === "cash_bonus");

    if (hasCash) return "Immediate cash bonus once earned.";
    if (hasVisibility) return "More exposure can increase bookings (not guaranteed).";
    if (hasFeeDiscount) return "Lower platform fees increase your take-home pay.";
    if (String(goal.metric_key ?? "").includes("addons")) return "Add-ons usually increase job earnings.";
    return null;
  }

  private async getPauseStatus(cleaner_id: string): Promise<{ paused: boolean; pause_reasons: string[] }> {
    return await withClient(async (client) => {
      const { rows } = await client.query(
        `SELECT paused, pause_reasons FROM cleaner_level_status WHERE cleaner_id=$1`,
        [cleaner_id]
      );
      if (!rows?.length) return { paused: false, pause_reasons: [] };
      return { paused: Boolean(rows[0].paused), pause_reasons: (rows[0].pause_reasons ?? []) as string[] };
    });
  }

  private async getCompletedGoalIds(cleaner_id: string): Promise<Set<string>> {
    return await withClient(async (client) => {
      const { rows } = await client.query(
        `SELECT goal_id FROM cleaner_goal_progress WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND status='complete'`,
        [cleaner_id]
      );
      return new Set((rows ?? []).map((r:any)=>String(r.goal_id)));
    });
  }

  private async getMetricSnapshot(cleaner_id: string): Promise<Record<string, any>> {
    // Assumes you store snapshot in cleaner_level_status.metric_snapshot (jsonb) from Step 6 persistence,
    // else this returns empty and Next Best Action will show zeros.
    return await withClient(async (client) => {
      const { rows } = await client.query(
        `SELECT metric_snapshot FROM cleaner_level_status WHERE cleaner_id=$1`,
        [cleaner_id]
      );
      const snap = rows?.[0]?.metric_snapshot ?? {};
      return snap;
    });
  }
}
""")

# Route: GET /cleaners/:id/next-best-actions
(routes/"next_best_actions.ts").write_text("""import { Router } from "express";
import { RuntimeConfigLoader } from "../services/runtime_config_loader";
import { DBConfigProvider } from "../services/config_provider";
import { SeasonService } from "../services/season_service";
import { NextBestActionService } from "../services/next_best_action_service";

export const nextBestActionsRouter = Router();

// Singletons
const loader = new RuntimeConfigLoader({ poll_ms: 120_000 });
loader.start();

const provider = new DBConfigProvider(loader);
const seasonService = new SeasonService();
const nbaService = new NextBestActionService(provider, seasonService);

// NOTE: region_id should come from cleaner profile or job market mapping.
// Here we allow query param for simplicity.
nextBestActionsRouter.get("/cleaners/:id/next-best-actions", async (req, res) => {
  const cleaner_id = req.params.id;
  const region_id = (req.query.region_id as string) ?? null;
  const limit = req.query.limit ? Number(req.query.limit) : 3;

  const result = await nbaService.getNextBestActions({ cleaner_id, region_id, limit });
  res.json({ ok: true, ...result });
});
""")

(services/"next_best_action_service.ts").write_text("""import { withClient } from "../db/client";
import { ConfigProvider } from "./config_provider";
import { SeasonService } from "./season_service";

/**
 * Step 16 — Next Best Action + Reward Preview
 *
 * Gives cleaners 1–3 concrete actions that move them closest to the next reward/level,
 * including reward preview chips and optional season boost indicators.
 *
 * Ranking heuristic:
 * 1) Core goals first
 * 2) Smallest remaining delta
 * 3) Higher level preferred if tie
 * 4) Higher cash value preferred if tie
 */

type AnyJson = Record<string, any>;

export type NextBestAction = {
  goal_id: string;
  level: number;
  goal_type: "core" | "stretch" | "maintenance";
  title: string;
  description: string;
  metric_key: string;
  target: number;
  current: number;
  remaining: number;
  percent_complete: number;
  reward_preview: Array<{
    reward_id: string;
    kind: string;
    label: string;
    duration_days?: number | null;
    amount_cents?: number | null;
    uses?: number | null;
  }>;
  earnings_impact_hint?: string | null;
  season_boosts?: Array<{ season_id: string; metric_key: string; multiplier: number }>;
};

export class NextBestActionService {
  constructor(
    private configProvider: ConfigProvider,
    private seasonService: SeasonService
  ) {}

  async getNextBestActions(params: {
    cleaner_id: string;
    region_id?: string | null;
    limit?: number;
  }): Promise<{ paused: boolean; pause_reasons: string[]; actions: NextBestAction[] }> {
    const cleaner_id = params.cleaner_id;
    const region_id = params.region_id ?? null;
    const limit = Math.min(Number(params.limit ?? 3), 10);

    const pause = await this.getPauseStatus(cleaner_id);
    if (pause.paused) {
      return { paused: true, pause_reasons: pause.pause_reasons, actions: [] };
    }

    const bundle = await this.configProvider.getBundle(region_id);
    const goals: AnyJson[] = (bundle.goals?.goals ?? bundle.goals ?? []) as AnyJson[];
    const rewards: AnyJson[] = (bundle.rewards?.rewards ?? bundle.rewards ?? []) as AnyJson[];

    const rewardsById = new Map<string, AnyJson>();
    for (const r of rewards) rewardsById.set(r.id, r);

    const metricSnapshot = await this.getMetricSnapshot(cleaner_id);

    const seasons = await this.seasonService.getActiveSeasons({ region_id, at: new Date() });
    const applied = this.seasonService.applyMultipliers({ metric_snapshot: metricSnapshot, seasons });

    const completedGoalIds = await this.getCompletedGoalIds(cleaner_id);

    const candidates = goals
      .filter(g => g && g.id && !completedGoalIds.has(String(g.id)))
      .filter(g => ["core","stretch","maintenance"].includes(String(g.type ?? g.goal_type ?? "core")))
      .map(g => this.toAction(g, rewardsById, applied.snapshot, applied.applied));

    const ranked = candidates.sort((a,b) => {
      const typeWeight = (t: string) => (t === "core" ? 0 : t === "stretch" ? 1 : 2);
      const tw = typeWeight(a.goal_type) - typeWeight(b.goal_type);
      if (tw !== 0) return tw;

      if (a.remaining !== b.remaining) return a.remaining - b.remaining;
      if (a.level !== b.level) return b.level - a.level;

      const cashVal = (x: NextBestAction) =>
        x.reward_preview.reduce((sum, r) => sum + (r.amount_cents ?? 0), 0);
      return cashVal(b) - cashVal(a);
    });

    return { paused: false, pause_reasons: [], actions: ranked.slice(0, limit) };
  }

  private toAction(goal: AnyJson, rewardsById: Map<string, AnyJson>, snapshot: AnyJson, season_boosts: any[]): NextBestAction {
    const level = Number(goal.level ?? goal.level_number ?? 1);
    const goal_type = String(goal.type ?? goal.goal_type ?? "core") as any;
    const metric_key = String(goal.metric_key ?? goal.metric?.key ?? goal.metric ?? "");
    const target = Number(goal.target ?? goal.metric?.target ?? 0);

    const current = Number(snapshot[metric_key] ?? 0);
    const remaining = Math.max(target - current, 0);
    const percent = target > 0 ? Math.min((current / target) * 100, 100) : (remaining === 0 ? 100 : 0);

    const rewardIds: string[] = goal.reward_ids ?? [];
    const reward_preview = rewardIds.map((rid) => {
      const r = rewardsById.get(rid) ?? {};
      const kind = String(r.kind ?? "unknown");
      return {
        reward_id: rid,
        kind,
        label: this.rewardLabel(r),
        duration_days: r.duration_days ?? null,
        amount_cents: r.params?.amount_cents ?? null,
        uses: r.uses ?? r.params?.uses ?? null
      };
    });

    const earnings_impact_hint = this.earningsHint(metric_key, reward_preview);

    const boostsForMetric = (season_boosts ?? [])
      .filter((x:any) => String(x.metric_key) === metric_key)
      .map((x:any) => ({ season_id: x.season_id, metric_key: x.metric_key, multiplier: x.multiplier }));

    return {
      goal_id: String(goal.id),
      level,
      goal_type,
      title: String(goal.title ?? goal.name ?? "Goal"),
      description: String(goal.description ?? ""),
      metric_key,
      target,
      current,
      remaining,
      percent_complete: Math.round(percent*10)/10,
      reward_preview,
      earnings_impact_hint,
      season_boosts: boostsForMetric.length ? boostsForMetric : undefined
    };
  }

  private rewardLabel(r: AnyJson): string {
    const kind = String(r.kind ?? "");
    if (kind === "cash_bonus") {
      const cents = Number(r.params?.amount_cents ?? 0);
      return cents > 0 ? `$${(cents/100).toFixed(0)} cash bonus` : "Cash bonus";
    }
    if (kind === "visibility_multiplier") {
      const m = Number(r.params?.multiplier ?? 1.0);
      const d = r.duration_days ? ` for ${r.duration_days} days` : "";
      return `Visibility boost x${m}${d}`;
    }
    if (kind === "early_exposure_minutes") {
      const mins = Number(r.params?.minutes ?? 0);
      const d = r.duration_days ? ` for ${r.duration_days} days` : "";
      return `Early exposure +${mins} min${d}`;
    }
    if (kind === "platform_fee_discount_percent") {
      const p = Number(r.params?.percent ?? 0);
      const d = r.duration_days ? ` for ${r.duration_days} days` : "";
      return `Platform fee -${p}%${d}`;
    }
    if (kind === "instant_payout_fee_waiver") {
      const uses = Number(r.params?.uses ?? 1);
      return `Instant payout fee waived (${uses} uses)`;
    }
    return String(r.label ?? r.name ?? kind || "Reward");
  }

  private earningsHint(metric_key: string, preview: Array<any>): string | null {
    const hasVisibility = preview.some(p => p.kind === "visibility_multiplier" || p.kind === "early_exposure_minutes");
    const hasFeeDiscount = preview.some(p => p.kind === "platform_fee_discount_percent");
    const hasCash = preview.some(p => p.kind === "cash_bonus");

    if (hasCash) return "Immediate cash bonus once earned.";
    if (hasVisibility) return "More exposure can increase bookings (not guaranteed).";
    if (hasFeeDiscount) return "Lower platform fees increase your take-home pay.";
    if (metric_key.includes("addons")) return "Add-ons usually increase job earnings.";
    return null;
  }

  private async getPauseStatus(cleaner_id: string): Promise<{ paused: boolean; pause_reasons: string[] }> {
    return await withClient(async (client) => {
      const { rows } = await client.query(
        `SELECT paused, pause_reasons FROM cleaner_level_status WHERE cleaner_id=$1`,
        [cleaner_id]
      );
      if (!rows?.length) return { paused: false, pause_reasons: [] };
      return { paused: Boolean(rows[0].paused), pause_reasons: (rows[0].pause_reasons ?? []) as string[] };
    });
  }

  private async getCompletedGoalIds(cleaner_id: string): Promise<Set<string>> {
    return await withClient(async (client) => {
      const { rows } = await client.query(
        `SELECT goal_id FROM cleaner_goal_progress WHERE cleaner_id=$1 AND status='complete'`,
        [cleaner_id]
      );
      return new Set((rows ?? []).map((r:any)=>String(r.goal_id)));
    });
  }

  private async getMetricSnapshot(cleaner_id: string): Promise<Record<string, any>> {
    // Preferred: store metric_snapshot jsonb on cleaner_level_status from Step 6.
    return await withClient(async (client) => {
      const { rows } = await client.query(
        `SELECT metric_snapshot FROM cleaner_level_status WHERE cleaner_id=$1`,
        [cleaner_id]
      );
      return (rows?.[0]?.metric_snapshot ?? {}) as any;
    });
  }
}
""")

(routes/"next_best_actions.ts").write_text("""import { Router } from "express";
import { RuntimeConfigLoader } from "../services/runtime_config_loader";
import { DBConfigProvider } from "../services/config_provider";
import { SeasonService } from "../services/season_service";
import { NextBestActionService } from "../services/next_best_action_service";

export const nextBestActionsRouter = Router();

const loader = new RuntimeConfigLoader({ poll_ms: 120_000 });
loader.start();

const provider = new DBConfigProvider(loader);
const seasonService = new SeasonService();
const nbaService = new NextBestActionService(provider, seasonService);

// region_id should come from cleaner profile / job market mapping.
// Here it can be supplied as query param for simplicity.
nextBestActionsRouter.get("/cleaners/:id/next-best-actions", async (req, res) => {
  const cleaner_id = req.params.id;
  const region_id = (req.query.region_id as string) ?? null;
  const limit = req.query.limit ? Number(req.query.limit) : 3;

  const result = await nbaService.getNextBestActions({ cleaner_id, region_id, limit });
  res.json({ ok: true, ...result });
});
""")

Core goals first

higher cash value preferred if tie

# SQL: Ops views for quick diagnosis
(sql_dir/"step17_ops_views_v1.sql").write_text("""-- Step 17 — Ops / Support diagnostic views (v1)
-- Assumes you have:
-- - cleaner_level_status (paused, pause_reasons, metric_snapshot)
-- - cleaner_goal_progress
-- - gamification_reward_grants / gamification_cleaner_active_rewards
-- - cleaner_badges / cleaner_achievement_feed
-- Optional: events table name can vary (see service), so views avoid it.

BEGIN;

-- Quick snapshot by cleaner
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ops_cleaner_gamification_snapshot AS
SELECT
  cls.cleaner_id,
  cls.level,
  cls.paused,
  cls.pause_reasons,
  cls.updated_at,
  (cls.metric_snapshot->>'rating.avg_stars')::numeric AS avg_stars,
  (cls.metric_snapshot->>'jobs.completed.lifetime')::bigint AS jobs_completed_lifetime
FROM cleaner_level_status cls;

-- Goals completion counts by level/type
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ops_cleaner_goal_counts AS
SELECT
  cleaner_id,
  MAX(level) AS current_level,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status='complete') AS goals_complete_total,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status='in_progress') AS goals_in_progress_total
FROM cleaner_goal_progress
GROUP BY cleaner_id;

-- Active rewards summary
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ops_cleaner_active_rewards AS
SELECT
  car.cleaner_id,
  array_agg(car.reward_id ORDER BY car.reward_id) AS active_reward_ids,
  COUNT(*) AS active_reward_count
FROM gamification_cleaner_active_rewards car
GROUP BY car.cleaner_id;

-- Recent achievements (feed)
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ops_cleaner_recent_achievements AS
SELECT
  cleaner_id,
  jsonb_agg(jsonb_build_object(
    'type', type,
    'ref_id', ref_id,
    'title', title,
    'created_at', created_at
  ) ORDER BY created_at DESC) AS recent
FROM (
  SELECT * FROM cleaner_achievement_feed
  ORDER BY created_at DESC
  LIMIT 5000
) t
GROUP BY cleaner_id;

COMMIT;
""")

# Support macros and in-app explainers
(ops_dir/"support_macros_v1.md").write_text("""# PureTask Support Macros — Gamification (v1)

These are copy-paste responses for support agents.

---

## 1) “Why is my progress paused?”
Your level stays the same — progress is only paused when a maintenance rule is not met.

**What to do next**
1) Open your “Maintenance” tab
2) Fix the item(s) listed (examples: acceptance rate, disputes, on-time)
3) Your progress resumes automatically once you’re back in compliance

If you want, tell me which pause reason you see and I’ll explain it.

---

## 2) “Why didn’t my message count?”
Messages count toward goals if they’re **meaningful**. A message counts if **any** of these are true:
- You used a Quick Template (Thank you / Reschedule / Review request / Tip request / On my way / Arrived / Finished)
- The message is **25+ characters**
- The client replied within 24 hours

If you send “ok”, “hi”, or short emojis, it won’t count.

---

## 3) “Why didn’t my login day/streak count?”
A login day only counts when you take at least **one meaningful action within 15 minutes** of opening the app:
- open a job request
- accept/decline a job
- send a meaningful message
- upload photos
- update availability

Opening and closing the app does not count.

---

## 4) “Why didn’t my photos count?”
Photo requirements are simple:
- at least **1 before** photo
- at least **1 after** photo
- timestamps must be **between clock-in and clock-out**

If the photos were taken outside the job window, progress won’t count.

---

## 5) “Why does my on-time % look wrong?”
You’re on-time if you clock in **15 minutes before to 15 minutes after** the scheduled start time **AND** within **250 meters** of the job location.

If GPS was outside 250m at clock-in, it will show as not on-time.

---

## 6) “Why did my acceptance rate drop?”
Some declines are **good-faith** and do not count against acceptance rate:
- distance too far (job is 11+ miles from your home base)
- outside your availability window
- job type mismatch (service you don’t offer)
- safety concern (photo optional, details required)
- access/logistics mismatch
- short notice (start time < 18 hours)

Non-good-faith declines count toward acceptance rate.

---

## 7) “What does priority visibility mean?”
Priority visibility does **not** guarantee bookings.

It means:
- you appear higher in the list when clients browse
- you may be shown earlier in the carousel
- you can get “early exposure” minutes where you show before others for a short window

Clients can still scroll and choose anyone.

---

## 8) “Cash bonuses are missing”
Cash bonuses can be paused if:
- the region cash budget cap is reached
- cash rewards are disabled temporarily (system safety)
- emergency rewards pause is active

Your goal completion still counts — cash bonuses will return when budgets reopen.

---
""")

# In-app explainers (cleaner-facing)
(docs_dir:=docs)
(docs_dir/"in_app_explainers_v1.md").write_text("""# In-app Explainers (Cleaner-facing) — v1 copy

## Progress Paused
**Progress paused (your level stays the same).**
Fix the maintenance items below to resume earning rewards and progress.

## Meaningful Login
A login day counts when you take **one meaningful action** within **15 minutes** of opening the app.

## Meaningful Message
A message counts if you use a Quick Template, write **25+ characters**, or the client replies.

## Photos
Photos count when you upload **1 before + 1 after**, taken **between clock-in and clock-out**.

## On-time
On-time means clocking in **15 minutes before to 15 minutes after** the start time, within **250 meters**.

## Acceptance Rate
Some declines are penalty-free when they’re good-faith (distance, availability, mismatch, safety, logistics, short notice).

## Visibility Rewards
Visibility boosts improve where you appear in the list. They never guarantee jobs.
""")

# Debug service: compute reasons why a metric didn't count (best-effort)
(services/"progress_debug_service.ts").write_text("""import { withClient } from "../db/client";

/**
 * ProgressDebugService (Step 17)
 *
 * Goal: provide support/admin with "why didn't it count?"
 *
 * This is best-effort and relies on events being present in an events table.
 * Because your event table name may vary across environments, we support ENV override:
 *  - EVENT_TABLE_NAME (default: 'event_store')
 *
 * Expected event payload fields (from Event Contract):
 * - event_name
 * - occurred_at
 * - payload jsonb
 *
 * The service returns recent events and derived diagnostics for:
 * - meaningful login
 * - meaningful message
 * - photo validity window
 * - on-time window + GPS radius
 */
type AnyJson = Record<string, any>;

export class ProgressDebugService {
  private eventTable = process.env.EVENT_TABLE_NAME || "event_store";

  async getCleanerDebug(params: { cleaner_id: string; job_id?: string; limit?: number }) {
    const { cleaner_id, job_id } = params;
    const limit = Math.min(Number(params.limit ?? 200), 500);

    const [status, recentGoals, recentRewards, recentFeed, recentEvents] = await Promise.all([
      this.getLevelStatus(cleaner_id),
      this.getRecentGoals(cleaner_id),
      this.getRecentRewards(cleaner_id),
      this.getRecentFeed(cleaner_id),
      this.getRecentEvents(cleaner_id, job_id, limit),
    ]);

    const diagnostics = this.buildDiagnostics(recentEvents, job_id);

    return {
      cleaner_id,
      status,
      recent_goals: recentGoals,
      recent_rewards: recentRewards,
      recent_achievements: recentFeed,
      recent_events: recentEvents,
      diagnostics
    };
  }

  private async getLevelStatus(cleaner_id: string) {
    return await withClient(async (client) => {
      const { rows } = await client.query(
        `SELECT cleaner_id, level, paused, pause_reasons, metric_snapshot, updated_at
         FROM cleaner_level_status
         WHERE cleaner_id=$1`,
        [cleaner_id]
      );
      return rows?.[0] ?? null;
    });
  }

  private async getRecentGoals(cleaner_id: string) {
    return await withClient(async (client) => {
      const { rows } = await client.query(
        `SELECT goal_id, status, progress_value, target_value, updated_at
         FROM cleaner_goal_progress
         WHERE cleaner_id=$1
         ORDER BY updated_at DESC
         LIMIT 50`,
        [cleaner_id]
      );
      return rows ?? [];
    });
  }

  private async getRecentRewards(cleaner_id: string) {
    return await withClient(async (client) => {
      const { rows } = await client.query(
        `SELECT reward_id, status, granted_at, ends_at, uses_remaining, source_type, source_id, meta
         FROM gamification_reward_grants
         WHERE cleaner_id=$1
         ORDER BY granted_at DESC
         LIMIT 50`,
        [cleaner_id]
      );
      return rows ?? [];
    });
  }

  private async getRecentFeed(cleaner_id: string) {
    return await withClient(async (client) => {
      const { rows } = await client.query(
        `SELECT type, ref_id, title, subtitle, created_at, meta
         FROM cleaner_achievement_feed
         WHERE cleaner_id=$1
         ORDER BY created_at DESC
         LIMIT 50`,
        [cleaner_id]
      );
      return rows ?? [];
    });
  }

  private async getRecentEvents(cleaner_id: string, job_id?: string, limit: number = 200) {
    // If your events table schema differs, update this one query.
    // The WHERE clause supports both cleaner_id and optional job_id inside payload.
    return await withClient(async (client) => {
      const table = this.eventTable;
      const args: any[] = [cleaner_id, limit];
      let where = `WHERE (payload->>'cleaner_id')::uuid = $1`;

      if (job_id) {
        args.splice(1, 0, job_id);
        where += ` AND (payload->>'job_id')::text = $2`;
        args[2] = limit;
      }

      const sql = `
        SELECT event_name, occurred_at, payload
        FROM ${table}
        ${where}
        ORDER BY occurred_at DESC
        LIMIT $${args.length}`;
      const { rows } = await client.query(sql, args);
      return rows ?? [];
    });
  }

  private buildDiagnostics(events: Array<{ event_name: string; occurred_at: string; payload: AnyJson }>, job_id?: string) {
    const diags: AnyJson = {};

    // Meaningful login: find session_started and meaningful_action within 15m
    const sessions = events.filter(e => e.event_name === "login_session_started");
    const actions = events.filter(e => e.event_name === "meaningful_action");
    if (sessions.length) {
      const s = sessions[0];
      const sTime = new Date(s.occurred_at).getTime();
      const hasAction = actions.some(a => {
        const aTime = new Date(a.occurred_at).getTime();
        return aTime >= sTime && aTime <= sTime + 15*60*1000;
      });
      diags.meaningful_login = {
        last_session_started_at: s.occurred_at,
        has_meaningful_action_within_15m: hasAction,
        note: hasAction ? "Counts as a login day if within the same calendar day." : "No meaningful action detected within 15 minutes."
      };
    }

    // Meaningful message: look at message_sent events
    const msgs = events.filter(e => e.event_name === "message_sent");
    if (msgs.length) {
      const m = msgs[0];
      const body = String(m.payload?.body ?? "");
      const template_id = m.payload?.template_id ?? null;
      const chars = body.length;
      const replied = Boolean(m.payload?.customer_replied_within_24h ?? false);
      const meaningful = Boolean(template_id) || chars >= 25 || replied;
      diags.meaningful_message = {
        last_message_at: m.occurred_at,
        template_id,
        chars,
        customer_replied_within_24h: replied,
        counted_as_meaningful: meaningful,
        note: meaningful ? "Counts toward messaging goals." : "Too short and not a template; does not count."
      };
    }

    // Photos: require 1 before + 1 after within clock window
    const clockIn = events.find(e => e.event_name === "clock_in");
    const clockOut = events.find(e => e.event_name === "clock_out");
    const photos = events.filter(e => e.event_name === "photo_uploaded");
    if (clockIn && clockOut && photos.length) {
      const start = new Date(clockIn.occurred_at).getTime();
      const end = new Date(clockOut.occurred_at).getTime();
      const before = photos.find(p => (p.payload?.kind ?? p.payload?.phase) === "before");
      const after = photos.find(p => (p.payload?.kind ?? p.payload?.phase) === "after");
      const beforeOk = before ? (new Date(before.occurred_at).getTime() >= start && new Date(before.occurred_at).getTime() <= end) : False;  # placeholder
""".replace("const beforeOk = before ? (new Date(before.occurred_at).getTime() >= start && new Date(before.occurred_at).getTime() <= end) : False;  # placeholder",
"""      const beforeOk = before ? (new Date(before.occurred_at).getTime() >= start && new Date(before.occurred_at).getTime() <= end) : false;
      const afterOk = after ? (new Date(after.occurred_at).getTime() >= start && new Date(after.occurred_at).getTime() <= end) : false;
      diags.photo_rules = {
        clock_in_at: clockIn.occurred_at,
        clock_out_at: clockOut.occurred_at,
        has_before: Boolean(before),
        has_after: Boolean(after),
        before_in_window: beforeOk,
        after_in_window: afterOk,
        counted: Boolean(beforeOk && afterOk),
        note: (beforeOk && afterOk) ? "Meets photo rule (1 before + 1 after within clock window)." : "Photos must be taken between clock-in and clock-out."
      };
    }

    // On-time + GPS radius (best-effort, depends on payload fields)
    if (clockIn) {
      const scheduled = clockIn.payload?.scheduled_start_at ? new Date(clockIn.payload.scheduled_start_at).getTime() : null;
      const actual = new Date(clockIn.occurred_at).getTime();
      const minsDiff = (scheduled != null) ? Math.round((actual - scheduled)/60000) : null;

      const gps_ok = (clockIn.payload?.gps_distance_meters != null)
        ? Number(clockIn.payload.gps_distance_meters) <= 250
        : null;

      const onTime = (minsDiff != null) ? (minsDiff >= -15 && minsDiff <= 15 && (gps_ok === null || gps_ok === true)) : null;

      diags.on_time = {
        scheduled_start_at: clockIn.payload?.scheduled_start_at ?? null,
        clock_in_at: clockIn.occurred_at,
        minutes_from_start: minsDiff,
        gps_distance_meters: clockIn.payload?.gps_distance_meters ?? null,
        gps_within_250m: gps_ok,
        counted_as_on_time: onTime,
        note: onTime === null
          ? "Scheduled start time not available in event payload."
          : (onTime ? "On-time by ±15 min rule and GPS radius." : "Late/early beyond ±15 min or GPS outside 250m.")
      };
    }

    // Good faith declines: explain most recent decline
    const declines = events.filter(e => e.event_name === "job_request_declined");
    if (declines.length) {
      const d = declines[0];
      diags.last_decline = {
        declined_at: d.occurred_at,
        reason: d.payload?.reason ?? null,
        good_faith: Boolean(d.payload?.good_faith ?? false),
        note: (d.payload?.good_faith ?? false) ? "Good-faith decline (does not affect acceptance rate)." : "Non-good-faith decline (affects acceptance rate)."
      };
    }

    return diags;
  }
}
"""))

(routes/"progress_debug.ts").write_text("""import { Router } from "express";
import { requireAdminRole } from "../middleware/rbac";
import { ProgressDebugService } from "../services/progress_debug_service";

export const progressDebugRouter = Router();
const svc = new ProgressDebugService();

/**
 * Support/admin: explain why progress did/didn't count
 * GET /admin/cleaners/:id/progress-debug?job_id=...&limit=200
 */
progressDebugRouter.get("/admin/cleaners/:id/progress-debug", requireAdminRole("support"), async (req, res) => {
  const cleaner_id = req.params.id;
  const job_id = (req.query.job_id as string) ?? undefined;
  const limit = req.query.limit ? Number(req.query.limit) : 200;

  const out = await svc.getCleanerDebug({ cleaner_id, job_id, limit });
  res.json({ ok: true, ...out });
});
""")

            except: pass
