can u explain how do to the things u request

Perfect — I’ll explain this step-by-step like you’ve never used Figma before.

You asked how to do the things I requested (Auto-layout, components, variants, grids, etc.). I’ll walk you through everything in simple language.

🟣 1️⃣ How To Create The Base Layout (App Shell)
Step 1 — Create a Desktop Frame
Open Figma

Press F (Frame tool)

On the right panel choose:

Desktop → 1440px

Rename it:

AppShell / Desktop
Step 2 — Add a 12 Column Grid
Click your frame

On right panel → Layout Grid

Click “+”

Change type to Columns

Set:

Count: 12

Margin: 80

Gutter: 24

Now you have a real app grid.

🟣 2️⃣ How To Create Sidebar + Main Layout
We use Auto Layout.

Think of Auto Layout like Flexbox in CSS.

Step 1 — Make Sidebar + Main Horizontal
Select the frame

Press Shift + A
→ This enables Auto Layout

Change direction to Horizontal

Now your frame splits into rows left-to-right.

Step 2 — Create Sidebar
Press R

Draw rectangle 240px wide

Rename: Sidebar

Set width = 240

Height = Fill container

Select sidebar → press Shift + A

Direction: Vertical

Padding: 24

Spacing: 16

Now it stacks items top-to-bottom.

Add:

Logo

Nav items

Step 3 — Create Main Content Area
Add another rectangle next to sidebar

Rename: Main Content

Set width to Fill container

Add Auto Layout:

Vertical

Padding: 48

Spacing: 32

Done. You now have a real app shell.

🟣 3️⃣ How To Make Reusable Components
You should NOT redesign buttons 50 times.

Example: Create Button Component
Draw rectangle (height: 44px)

Add text inside (“Primary Button”)

Select both

Press Shift + A (Auto layout)

Set:

Padding: 16 left/right

Height: Hug contents

Add corner radius: 8px

Add background color

Now:

Press Cmd/Ctrl + Alt + K

This turns it into a Component.

Rename:

Button / Primary
Create Variants (Very Important)
Select the component

Click “+ Variant”

Change property to:

Primary

Secondary

Danger

Disabled

Now you have a real system.

🟣 4️⃣ How To Make GoalCard Component
Step 1 — Create Card Frame
Create frame 600px wide

Add:

Title text

Progress bar

Small description

Button row

Select everything

Press Shift + A

Vertical

Padding: 24

Spacing: 16

Add:

Radius: 16

Fill: White

Shadow

Turn into component:

GoalCard
Add Variants:
Core

Stretch

Maintenance

Completed

Locked

Use Figma Variant property panel.

🟣 5️⃣ How To Make Tabs
Create horizontal frame

Add 4 text labels

Select all → Shift + A

Spacing: 32

Add underline element under active tab

Make this a component:

Tabs / Default
Variants:

Active tab index 1

Active tab index 2

etc

🟣 6️⃣ How To Make Circular Progress (Level Progress)
Two ways:

Easy Way (Recommended)
Draw circle

Duplicate

Change stroke color

Use “Arc” property in right panel

Adjust angle to represent %

Inside add:

63%

“To Level 5”

Group and make component:

LevelProgressRing
🟣 7️⃣ How To Create Cards With Consistent Spacing
Always:

Use Auto Layout

Use padding multiples of 8

Use spacing multiples of 8

Use consistent radius (8 or 16)

Never manually drag things into place.

🟣 8️⃣ How To Create Clean Admin Tables
Create frame

Make top row header

Add Auto Layout horizontal

Add column labels

Duplicate row

Create Component:

DataTable / Row
Make row variant:

Normal

Hover

Selected

🟣 9️⃣ How To Make Clean Design System Page
Create a new page in Figma:

Design System
Add sections:

Buttons

Cards

Typography

Colors

Badges

GoalCard

RewardCard

NavItem

MetricCard

Organize with big section headers.

🟣 🔟 How To Make It Clickable (Prototype Mode)
Click “Prototype” tab in right panel

Select button

Drag blue node to target frame

Set:

On Click

Navigate to

Smart Animate

Now you have clickable wireframes.

🧠 Important Concept
Auto Layout = Flexbox
Variants = Component states
Frame = Page container
Hug contents = dynamic size
Fill container = flexible size

Frame

Components

You can build any product.
